title: Accurate and effective - what a tool should be
content: I purchased these clamping square guides to help with creating face frames for a cabinet as well as for the purposes of simple right angle guides for checking squareness of joinery.The right angle thick aluminum guides measured true square against my known-good framing square and are thick enough that they should take quite a bit of stress before deforming. The included clamps (4 in total) are easy to position and tighten down, though I found myself using all 4 clamps on a single corner for extra stability hold. There's not much else to say about these except they do what they are supposed to and are true square. If I had to nit-pick one item it would be the length of the clamping bolts - I would like to have a bit more reach to support larger dimensions of wood, but then these brackets aren't sized for larger 4x4 or similar framing - not the fault of the kit as it's not sized for it - this is definitely more for cabinet framing, picture framing, and the like.I also liked that everything came in recessed foam slots in the box so that nothing collided or moved around during shipping. I'd make this purchase again.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Very good quality, 180 pieces, 162 magnetic bits, high quality case.
content: This is an excellent, high quality bit kit with a very good variety of sizes and styles of precision style bits. Everything from the case, bits and driver (note that it’s not powered, but manual) is good quality. It has a better case and more bits in it than other sets I’ve seen, it’s a little pricier than some kits but well worth it and a good value. It has 180 total pieces in the set with 162 precision, magnetic bits.Note again that the driver in it is not electric, it’s manual, hand powered. There is no power to the driver, nor does it use a battery or is it chargeable, it’s just a very nice standard hand powered driver to use with the bits.Note that the bits are more meant in size, style, shape for smaller items, electronics, appliances than larger standard household and construction size and style bits. There are some standard bits in it, but you’ll likely want to save this kit for more precise and specialized items. I save this kit for specialized items, and have other kits for standard household use so I don’t have to use the standard bits in this kit, and have them there if I need them on items that use both specialized and standard bits.Note that the foam inserts in it are just to get it there safely, they should be removed. You don’t need to leave them in the kit, it’s hard to close with them in the kit. Do be sure to lay the kit flat on a table when you first open it, as some of the bits (only one was for me) can be stuck in the foam and need to be put back in the case. If you try to save the foam inserts, it’s going to be very hard to close the case. It’s designed to be used and opened and closed without the foam inserts, so go ahead and dispose of them. If you note, none of the listing pictures show it with the foam inserts left in. The bits will stay in securely without the foam inserts.The case both looks nice and functions well in holding the bits. It’s easy to open and close securely (if you remove the foam inserts that aren’t meant to be left in). Note that the top of the case has a removable magnetic piece. It does remove fully, so be careful not to loose it when you open and close the case. I do think that the case stays closed without it, so if you did loose it, it would still close securely. The case is relatively small for all that’s in it and the design was well thought out to utilize the space inside the case. There are marking labels for each bit, but do note it’s fairly tiny on the print.

content: Update: I bought a second set of 4 of these. I checked the outside edge for square against my 6" precision machinist square and these aluminum pieces a perfect square at least by human eyes and touch. I haven't yet tested the interior angle for square, however.I now have 8 of these so I can glue up 4 table legs overnight, saving time and money.Original: I did not have great success with the supplied clamps so I tried my own spring clamps then I tried to add friction to the supplied clamps by shrink tubing the supplied steel rods. The latter stopped the slippage, but the rids still torqued the braces off their flush postuon, rendering that solution useless when trying to square legs. However for this project, the best solution was some cheap harbor freight clamps. I will possibly buy a second set so I can do 4 legs/ or 4 corners at one time. These are attractive right angle bars. I will update my review if I find a good way to successfully use the supplied clamps. I'm sure I'll eventually find some other use for them.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Works Very Well
content: Ordinarily I'm not a big fan of fancy gadgets replacing simple tools that work perfectly well, but this laser/measuring tape combo is winning me over.Firstly, the standard measuring tape works just fine and is actually very nice quality. The lock and retention are effortless and the mini magnets mounted into the hook actually stay put on steel when you draw out the tape. The tape is marked on both sides. The top side has both inches and metric. The inches are in 1/16" increments. On the bottom the metric is the same as the top, but the inches are not in 1/32 increments, which I think is very handy and makes it easier to read. The tape retracts smoothly.The laser measurement works very well and is pretty easy to use, once you go through the included instruction manual. One reviewer complained he couldn't access calibration mode, which I had problems with at first, too. The manual gives the impression you should hold down both the "off/clear" button and also the "meas" button simultaneously. That doesn't work.To get into laser calibration mode, hold down the "off/clear" button and just press the "meas" button once. Voila -- you're in calibration mode. Hope that helps someone who's having difficulty.The many functions of the laser are easy to get to and pretty clear to know which is which from the simple display. I haven't played with all of them yet, but if you just need the basic linear function, you don't even need the manual to figure it out.Overall, this measuring tool feels very solid. It's useful and not just a cheap gimmick. I'm not sure if it would survive a fall off a 10' ladder, but I'd say that it's about as sturdy as they can make it for the price. Sure, it could be made indestructable, but then it would probably also cost $500. I'm pretty careful with tools in general and don't throw them around, so for me, I think this will be fine.Lastly, this is surprisingly compact, considering it combines a measuring tape and laser distance unit in one. The belt clip is good, and it comes with a wrist strap, with helps to protect from accidental drops. I really like the fact that it is USB-C rechargeable. The battery seems to last a long time. I haven't emptied it yet.Overall, I think this will be a very handy tool to have once I learn all its features. But even as just a plain old measuring tape, it's quite good.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Tactile rugged feel, clear screen, accurate readings.
content: Many features in a tiny easy to use old school package. If you are not familiar with these kinds of measurements, I would suggest reading the manual as that can help, but you also need to have some understanding of how things work for measurement, what kind of measurements are important, and how to use these types of gadgets to save you time in general. This feels like the your old school tape measures, but packed with tech to make your life a lot easier, if you know what you are doing. The buttons have a tactile feel, and it charges using a USB C charging slot which should be default for components these days, but you would be surprised. It has a rugged rubber coated utilitarian design with the simple big buttons on the side that are just in the right place for my fingers to find them easily. The tape measure retraction button is easy to press and and has a powerful pull back reaction trigger. The screen is easy to see at the steepest angles away from you with almost unnoticeable artefacts. The font on the digital screen is similar to your basic calculator font and easy to read, because most everyone is used to these kinds of fonts on calculators and digital watches. Overall, a quality product that leaves little to desire for.This review is for the “Laser Tape Measure 197Ft Laser Measurement  16Ft Manual Measuring Digital Tape Measure Support to Pythagorean Mode, Area, Volume, Ft/Ft+in/in/M Unit Distance Meter for Woodworker ToolLaser Tape Measure 197Ft Laser Measurement  16Ft Manual Measuring Digital Tape Measure Support to Pythagorean Mode, Area, Volume, Ft/Ft+in/in/M Unit Distance Meter for Woodworker Tool”.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Very handy set with a very versatile ratcheting "T" handle and a nice case
content: The storage box is attractive and stays shut with a strong magnetic draw between the lid and bottom of case. I appreciate that it is labeled on the top as to what is in this case since I end up with so many little tool kits and don't want to open everyone to see what's inside.The long bits are clipped in on the lid and stay put with a plastic latch for each. One clip showed up broken, so I have a loose bit in my box. It is a shame because it was perfect otherwise. The lower bits have some sort of clip in the bottom of the hole that engages the bit tightly to keep it in place once pressed all the way. This is an awesome idea because many sets like this repeatedly have the bits all come loose and make a jumbled mess inside every time you open to use.The bits seem like a good quality metal and work well for each different type of fastener I was able to try. There is a good selection of sizes to cover many of the daily things I need bits for. Each different type of short bit is color coded which makes working easier once you identify what type of fastener you are working with. The case is also labeled well with each bit marked on the case so you can keep order and find a size quicker.The ratchet handle is great. Not only does it seem to function well, it also gives you a lot of added torque with the "T" design making is much easier to use than a regular handled screwdriver. The handle ends come out of each side allowing you to use them as extensions for the mani bit or use them individually as a pen type driver you could use with your fingers. This could be beneficial in tight places.It is hard to get excited about a bit set as they seem like something so normal and plain but some of the designs of this one set it above the rest to me. The magnetic closure built into the case, the securing action in the bottom of each small bit's hole, and the versatile ratcheting handle really make this one a winner for me.

product.name: The Almighty Ratchet Wrench™ Set 42 in 1

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Miniature Cordless Drill
content: This is one sweet kit. The drill itself is very small, about the size of a pen. It does weigh more than a ball point pen, but it's still pretty lightweight. The aluminum case is well designed. It uses a magnetic closure and it doesn't open accidentally. You push in one end and it pops out a little and you pull it open. The drill sits in it using friction to stay in place. The 10 drill bits are held in place with magnets keeping them in their slots. All the drill bits are labeled with their size (which ranges from .6 to 2.2 mm). There are also 10 additional magnetic slots for additional bits (I don't know where to get additional bits though). It has 4 speeds and ot says it can reach a top speed of 700rpm. It is only a drill so it only spins clockwise. I think if they decide to make a 2nd version, they could let it also spin counter clockwise and include some small Phillips and flat screwdriver bits. But this is a great miniature drill.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: This kit is SO COOL. (I'm such a geek...)
content: Amazingly versatile kit with every imaginable mini bit and a handful of small nut drivers, plus a few slightly larger ones with converters, extenders, and even a magnet for picking things up!The 180 count includes the case, and there are a small number of double/triple bits (for the most popular ones that are most likely to be lost or worn down from lots of use - I count three Y 0.8 bits, two each of U 2.0 and U2.6) - but I count that as a bonus, since I can't stand when I need a spare and don't have it!The case is very cleverly made. It takes a bit of gripping to pry the two halves open, don't be afraid to wrench at it a bit. There are 2 sets of magnets that hold it closed, the little square gold/brass colored bit is held on to both sides with 4 small magnets and is more cosmetic than functional (but it still looks cool). The sliding gold/brass colored bit on the side of the screwdriver handle doesn't actually do anything other than look cool and cover up 3 small magnets (I assume useful for holding bits or screws on the screwdriver while you are .. using the screwdriver?). There were a few things that I thought looked like they ought to be functional but are just cosmetic. That's ok, it's still a heck of a set.The screwdriver itself is static. There's no automated anything. It doesn't spin, or ratchet, or rotate. As advertised, the flat sides mean it won't go rolling away. When I place the butt end of the screwdriver handle in the palm of my hand it's super easy to use my fingers to spin it in either direction, very ergonomic and comfortable.The kit comes with two 5-packs of flat openers to pry open things like laptop cases and phones and remote controls, two pairs of tweezers, and two other thicker crowbar type openers. Lots of tools for getting inside all those things that are a pain to get inside of.And this thing looks SO COOL!

title: great for miniatures/40k
content: I like to magnetize my models and end up using a pin vise to set tiny neodymium magnets in the plastic to swap arms/ weapons.This little tool has made that process so much quicker. The sharp point bits easy and stay in the place I want to drill even on small 28mm scale minis I use at least in ABS plastic. It did take some getting used to the buttons for use but once you drill a few items then it is second nature.I find in plastic metal bits basically never wear out and these seem like good quality.The light on the front is a life saver. It makes drilling into barrels or holes to sink magnets a breeze.For charging it is standard USB-C which is awesome.The case is a bit odd, it is larger than it needs to be. I put the drill bits with a hook and loop tie around them and threw it away. It looks great, and it does organize everything, but its a lot of wasted space with no room to put more bits or anything else in there. As a gift box though its impressive and if you have the storage space (it is not very big) it does keep things tidy. I just wanted to keep this in a thinner footprint.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Good build quality - feature packed - rechargeable
content: This is a laser tape measure (197ft) and manual tape measure (16ft) combo. The body is made of matte plastic, has a backlit LCD screen on the top, a laser emitter and sensor at the front, and contains a traditional manual tape measure. It also has a clip for your belt.The build quality is quite good; it feels like it could survive being dropped, except for the screen. I suspect that’s the weakest point. The screen is recessed, so it would be protected in a fall, but I’d still treat it nicer than my other tape measures.I tested the accuracy at several distances, and as far as I can tell, it’s as accurate or more accurate than I can measure manually. I did notice it had trouble with consistency with anything less than 10cm, but really anything less than that should be measured with a ruler or caliper.Here are some of the features which may come in handy:Has a calibration function (but I didn’t need to use it)Has functions like Pythagorean, adding or subtracting measurements… etc.Easy to figure out without the manual (except for calibration)Remembers the last 4 measurementsBacklit LCD screenBeep can be turned offMarkings in metric and imperial on both sides of the tapeMagnets at the end of the tape to help hold itTurns off after a couple of minutes of non-use to save batteryOverall, I’m very happy with this measuring tape. I like how it combines the two types of tape measures, it has many useful features, and uses rechargeable batteries (I dislike single-use batteries). Recommended.

title: Neat little drill pen, great for small scale hobby use around markerspace workbench
content: Two of my hobbies are electronics design/repair and 3D printing. Both at times require some tiny, precision drilling. I have a Dremel I use but it's bulky and not terribly precise unless mounted in my Dremel drill press fixture, which defeats the portability. I have a manual pin vise I use for micro-drilling when needed but it's... manual. Hey, I'm an engineer and I like my power tools!I have a bit of a screwdriver addition. I can't explain it, but I have a LOT of screwdrivers, both manual and powered, including several electric screwdrivers of similar formfactor to this drill pen. At least one of them even has a little 1/8" chuck adapter for drill bits, but they just don't have much torque and so I never tried to use them for drilling.Seeing this one was dedicated to drilling, I thought I'd give it a look. I'm properly impressed at the power this little drill pen has, as well as the number of bits (with handy little plastic collars that actually have the size printed on it rather than just color coded) it comes with in its compact aluminum pop-out case. It charges via USB-C like most of my other electric micro screwdrivers, and battery life is reasonable despite drilling typically requiring more torque and power than simply driving screws.This is a very handy little device for makers!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Work great. Accuracy is spot on
content: This measuring tape has been a great addition to my tool bag. I was a little worried at first, because why overcomplicate something as simple as a measuring tape? But honestly, this tape is great to use.First, it's very well built. It's sturdy construction has so far held up to the light abuse I've put it through. It doesn't feel cheap in your hand. The belt clip is nice and strong, and the wrist strap adds an additional method of keeping this tape at hand. It also feels like it's reasonably water resistant, so there shouldn't be too much issue using it in the rain. I just wouldn't fully submerge it in water.The technology in this tape is a little complicated to wrap your head around for the first little while. However, reading the included manual is a major help. It is written in pretty clear english, but there are some signs that this could have been translated. Some of the wording is just a bit off, but the instructions are good enough to understand how to use it.In my experience, the laser measure is just as accurate as the manual tape. This enables a world of different types of measurements using the built-in calculation features. It's also a nice feature that you can select if you're measuring to the front or back of the tape body. The body is also stamped with it's fixed size, in case you're using the manual tape for measurements. The final point to comment on is the manual tape hook end. It hook actually slides to make sure the zero reference is the same if you're using it butted up against something or hooked over an edge.While this measuring tape is a little on the steep side price-wise, it does feel like it's priced right for the value. I'm glad I've added it to my kit!

title: Good for small-to-medium tasks!
content: This is a nice, compact electric blower. It's handheld and is about 5" tall. You can run it in 4 different speeds, with the highest speed requiring that you hold the button down. The blower is powerful enough to blow the leaves off a porch - not as fast as a full-size leaf blower, but still enough to be functional. It's better for smaller particles, like sawdust or grass. It handles dust very well and works for keyboards, computers, electronics, and the like.You get 2 nose cones for it, or you can use it without. They attach magnetically and work well.On the high setting, it does produce a high-pitched whine that I personally find annoying, but I do have sensitive hearing. That's only an issue indoors on the high setting - outside it's fine and on lower settings it's fine. It's worth it to me to just pop some earplugs in if I have to use it.The runtime varies based on the speed setting (see the photo I took of the manual.) The times I got matched up with those.I think this is a good tool that is easy to use for cleaning off debris or dust, or for drying things. It seems well-built and like it will last quite a while.

title: Extraordinary in all ways. Impressive. I mean...really impressive....
content: A Mini Electric Drill Pen.A Workbench Drill Press.I couldn't resist.As an avid Do-It-Yourself'er with an extensive shop, I simply couldn't resist picking this up, I mean...a Mini Electric Drill Pen. How cool is that! In addition, I'm actually a Model Car Kit enthusiast, as I build, assembly, glue and paint model cars, particularly muscle cars from the late 1960's and early 1970's, (Revell, AMT, MPC, Atlantis, and Moebuis model car kits).These kits, made out of styrene, typically need some rework to the body during assembly. Drilling a tiny hole out for the door locks, side mirror, gas cap, etc is quite common, and done by hand.Never again, since I've got this impressive drill pen. A mini drill, with a 10 pc. mini drill bit set, all various sizes, powered by USB, and an a built-in LED light....I can go on and on! I love this thing!-Electric Mini Drill Pen-Aluminum Case-Impressive Mini Drill Bench-10 pc Drill Bit Set-Benchtop Drill Press-USB-C CableI am most likely to use the Pen by hand, instead of using the Drill Press, however, setting up the drill press was a snap. Had it assembled in a few minutes. Everything is provide, (except the mounting bolts for the drill press to your workbench).The instructions were clear, good diagrams and very easy to follow. Most of this is so intuitive. There is no learning curve. What an impressive tool and addition to my shop!Could not be happier, and certainly I'm blown away by the quality, performance, asthetics, (are a killer), material, the case, drill set....everything is so well made.For $113, you get what you pay for. This is not some cheap Harbor Freight tool. This istruly an unparalleled and unwavering Precision Mini Electric Drill that has literally blown me away, as it truly is a stand out.Love the LED Illumination so I can execute accuracy and efficiency, when performing rework on my model car kit builds.Overall, I am forever impressed! I cannot find a weakness anywhere! This is a beauty, and should last a lifetime.Simply remarkable!

title: this is for more delicate work. I use it for making PCB's
content: I was expecting junk... I was wrong. Very pleasantly surprised. From time to time I make Printed circuit boards. They're relatively easy to make, but the part they don't tell you is that you have to drill the holes for through hole components yourself. One or two is no big deal, but when you have 50 holes to drill, it becomes a chore. At that scale accuracy is a necessity, and a regular drill press makes it accurate, but setting up for each hole can be a chore.This has a metal frame, and a decent drill for small items like pc boards. I could even see it useful for leather, plastic, jewelry, resin, it has a lot of possibilities and it works well. This is not intended for woodworking or other big jobs, this is for more delicate work. It doesn't have the power to drill easily through steel (although it can drill through it), this is more of a precision instrument.It seems a little pricey, but I think it's more of an investment vs a purchase.

As they are made of fairly thick metal, they are study enough to support fairly large and heavy pieces of sheet products (plywood or particle board types of products) that sit upright while being secured to horizontal pieces (I've been using them to join pieces of 3/4 inch sheet products that measure roughly 30 inches square). The purpose of these is to get 2 pieces oriented to the square of each other, not necessarily to apply lateral clamping force. Therefore, in making a box or cabinet carcass, you will still need something like a bar clamp to apply that force.

title: Great way to remove dust and dry items on the spot - Highly Recommended!
content: I recently received the Cordless Air Duster 130,000RPM High-Speed Mini Blower, and I’m really impressed with its performance. When it first arrived, it came nearly zero charged, but the USB-C charging made it quick and easy to power up. After charging, I was able to test out its three different air flow settings—low, medium, and high—and it provided ample power for whatever task I needed.One feature I absolutely love is how the cones attach by magnets. The magnets are surprisingly strong, yet they don’t require much effort to remove, and once attached, the cones stay in place even if you bump the tip or put it down. Personally, I’ve only been using the narrow cone, and it works great for my needs. The airflow isn’t quite the same as compressed air cans, but it’s still more than powerful enough for most tasks. It’s not as harsh as canned air, which is a plus in my book.I’ve used it to blow debris off the windows it got the job done. It works perfectly fine for tasks like cleaning electronics or cooling off a pet. The key thing to keep in mind is that while it’s effective, it’s not suitable for extremely delicate tasks—so I wouldn’t use it too close to sensitive items like eyes, ears, or paintings. But for general use, it’s fantastic.One of the main reasons I love this air duster is its cost-effectiveness over time. Unlike traditional air cans, there’s no need to buy replacements, and there’s no condensation build-up. Plus, it’s lightweight and portable, making it easy to take with me wherever I go. I’ve even used it for small paint jobs on models and touch-ups around the house. The versatility of this mini blower is impressive—it really has a wide range of uses.The 4000mAh battery offers long-lasting wireless operation, and with the fast USB-C charging, I never have to wait long for a power-up. The airflow is powerful enough for all my dusting needs, and the fact that it’s easy to store and travel with makes it a perfect addition to my toolkit.In conclusion, this cordless air duster is a must-have for anyone looking for an affordable, long-lasting alternative to compressed air cans. Whether you’re cleaning electronics, cooling off your pet, or just tidying up your space, this mini blower gets the job done efficiently and effectively. I highly recommend it!

content: I've purchased a handful of dusters throughout the decade, and many if not all of them have been anemic. The Almighty Hurricane is not anemic. title: Great quality tape but read the instructions carefully

product.name: The Almighty Tape Measure™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: This laser tape measure is a must have
content: This should be the future of all tape measures.Having both a laser measure and a manual tape measure is a joy.Especially for any real estate agents out there - this is a must-have!Every real estate agent I have come into contact with will always have a separate laser measure (like the Dewalt I am showing and comparing to) and a manual tape measure in their show-and-tell toolkit.The tape measure comes in a standard box with minimal tape measure features printed on it.Include is the tape measure, USB-C charging cord, attached wrist strap and a very comprehensive and detailed instruction manual.As like all typical laser measures it has all the same available features like audio on/off, backlight on/off, measuring metrics in meters/inches/feet, 4-sided area, total volume, auto calc pythagorean measure, total distance, height.The tape itself is quite robust, all metal, typical of what you would see from any brand name measuring tape.The tape itself has a pull-out auto-break which is nice with a solid tape retraction with no slack after pressing the retraction button.The retraction is so good that even pulling the tape out a tiny tiny bit such as 1/4 of an inch, then pressing the retract button retracts the tape back into the housing.That's when you know this is very well-made.The tape end hook has a firm solid rubber overmold which is fantastic as this make this non-marking, plus 2 tiny magnets to help the hook stay put when manually measuring longer distances.The entire tape measure is made out of ABS plastic with again, rubber overmolds over the 5 buttons.The LCD display has a slight arched frame around it to prevent it from being scratched.The LCD display lens looks like it's made out of thin plexi so best to be careful anyways as I can see this being scratched if tossed in a tool box with other tools or nails/screws.Laser itself has a reach of 197 feet. That's 97 feet longer than my dedicated Dewalt laser.Tape measure length is only 16 feet with both imperial and metric measures.I did some extensive (unscientific) measure comparisons to check for laser accuracy with my usual go-to Dewalt laser as I have shown above in my photos.I performed short measurements and long measurements and this tape measure was bang on everytime give or take maybe 1mm or so.The only feature I wish this tape measure had would be a readout for the actual extraction of the manual tape. So if I pulled the tape out to 20 inches, the LCD would read 20 inches. And have the reading adjust as I move the tape in and out. Similar to how a caliper works. But no worries, as that's no big deal for such a good tool.Lastly, the Dewalt laser cost me just over $100 when I bought it about over a year ago.This laser tape measure is a bargain for all the features it has, plus having to carry one less tool.Did I say this is a must-have yet?
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Tape Measure™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: More than just your average tape measure...
content: It's an interesting combination of a traditional tape measure and a laser-based area measuring tool. The clearly marked tape can handle anything up to 16-feet, with the laser then capable of giving a reading to nearly 200. But then you can also get into all the math functions, with various ways of calculating volume and space, which may be a little beyond the average user's needs, though you never know when you'll need to come up with a quick estimate on how big a room is...The body seems to be built to take a certain amount of use / abuse, though I definitely wouldn't want to drop it from any sort of height (simply due to the electronics inside).

You can tell that they care about their products and how it arrives. The instructions were full color, and it came with a thank-you note from the founder of the company. Nice touch! The duster has some weight to it. Not the kind of weight that makes it hard to use, but the kind that lets you know that it's built with quality. It doesn't feel cheap or hollow, its solid. I turned it on and was amazed at how powerful it was. It looks like a jet engine turbine from the intake and it blows air like one, too. It is VERY strong. It had no issues whatsoever blowing out the dust from my computer on the 2nd of 3 settings. Battery life is great, I was able to get my entire PC cleaned out top to bottom, and still had 4/5 lights on the indicator left. I love that it's small enough to put in my tech bag too.

title: A powered pump you can actually take with you!
content: As any cyclist knows, being able to change a tire / tube, or inflate one, out on the road is an essential part of biking, especially when any serious distance is concerned. While manual / hand pumps get the job done, they also sap precious energy that could otherwise be used pedaling, lol.The main problem with taking a battery powered pump is typically WEIGHT. Even my smaller (relatively speaking) pumps are pretty cumbersome when I'm the source of locomotive energy that moves them along with myself on this big marble floating in space.This pump, however, is, in no uncertain terms, small enough to actually go out on rides with you. It's light enough that you genuinely won't notice the mass of it on your next hill climb. It's fast enough that you be SO glad you left that hand pump at home. It does Schraeder OR Presta valves. And it's set it and forget it, it stops right where it needs to.Obviously, being this small its battery life is limited. You won't be pumping up a dozen 26" fat tires with this, forget it. But it has plenty of battery for a tire or 2, and that's enough to get you home.I've been wanting a mini pump that was ACTUALLY a mini pump that you can ACTUALLY take with you. This definitely fits the bill! It works great, and I definitely recommend it. 5 stars!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Great little driver
content: So far I've only used this to work on a couple Xbox controllers, but it's great for that. I don't work on a ton of electronics, but this is sure great to have. I had another littler set, crappy case, not a lot of torque to loosen the screws. This one works great, I like the case, it looks like something out of Star Wars. There are a lot of bits, a lot I probably won't actually use, but who knows. I was a little worried about the case being held closed by a magnet, but it's strong and even falling off the arm of the couch, it never moved.I like that the diameter of this driver is bigger than the other one I had, it's easier to hold for me, and the buttons are within easy reach. Changing the needed power level is also easy, make sure you don't over torque things. I also like that it comes with sturdier case separating tools than the tools that came with the new joy sticks. They have held up well, and there's plenty of them to slide in and keep the case open until you can get it apart. There's also plenty of extensions if you need to work with deep set screws.I can't say much for how long the battery lasts, but it held up through working on the 2 controllers. Overall, I really like this screwdriver set. Nice compact, easy to store package that keeps everything together. The only downside was the excessive packaging when it arrived.
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Screwdriver Set 180 in 1™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: This is a nice kit that would make a great gift
content: This Precision Screwdriver Set comes 162 bits in most of the interfaces one would encounter when working on today’s electronic devices. The bits work on other things too, like glasses and cameras. The electric driver is smooth and powerful enough to do the job without stripping out the screws. There is even a three setting “gear control" that varies the maximum torque to help keep the most delicate screws intact.The end of the driver is magnetized so it holds bits in place, and also applies a bit of magnetization to hold onto screws. This is a great feature in that it keeps screws and bits from falling into the machine you are working on, as well as helping position screws when putting things back together.The driver's battery charges in about an hour using the now ubiquitous USB as a charging source. I plugged mine into a USB-C data cable hanging off of my PC to test it out. It seems to hold a pretty decent charge. The listing says something about tightening 1000 screws, but I don’t know if they mean on a single charge or if the whole thing will turn to dust at that point. My guess is they were talking about the battery life. That being said, the battery in the driver doesn’t look to be replaceable so the driver will eventually give out.The kit comes in a nice fitted case with magnetic catches to keep it closed. These magnets are plenty strong enough to do their job. The box itself is sturdy and does a pretty good job of holding all of the bits in place while you tote it from job to job. It comes packed with these two “finger” foam packing strips. I’ve put those back in the box for their additional capability to keep all of the bits in place. I really hate losing bits and having every bit in its place tells me they are all there.The kit includes some spreaders and such to pry open laptop cases and other devices that snap together. They look to be adequate, but I didn’t try them out. I have a laptop spreader kit that I really like. However, if I didn’t already have spreaders I wouldn’t hesitate to use these.The overall quality and precision of the kit is good. Compared to the other precision screwdriver sets I have, the bits from this set fit screws better. The push buttons on the driver have a good solid feel, and the driver itself runs smoothly in both directions. As I mentioned, even the hard plastic case is made with a decent amount of precision, which in a world of tools coming in blow-molded plastic is a blessing.With all of that going for it I’ve got to give this 5-stars, even though there are a couple of oddities. These oddities are really minor, and don’t affect functionality. The first oddity is the marking on the buttons. It is marked with an “O” for in and an “X” for out. That is backwards from convention. (Imagine looking at the back of an arrow versus the tip of an arrow and this should make sense) The other oddity is the switch thingy that they call the “Anti-Anxiety Magnetic Slider”. I can’t for the life of me tell what that thing does. Maybe it’s just a fidget tool.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: For the price it does quite well.
content: I am leveling ceilings and floors in a 80+ years old house. I knew the ceilings had low spots so I needed to locate them. This laser tool got that done. Keep in mind there is an offset when leveling floors. It is about 3/8 of an inch and that is due to the offset due to the base. This unit I ordered came with several different mounting jigs and a remote. The remote did not work, however in the manual I found instructions how to pair the remote with the laser assembly. Now the remote works fine. The laser light beam in not a tight focused beam. At about 10 foot the beam itself becomes 3/16 of inch wide. That is relatively minor as I could work with that. Overall the unit works great and far far cheaper than those made by Bosch which are $500 or more. The laser beam is visible in low light. Not all that visible in bright light. Not visible beyond a few feet in bright sunlight Overall this laser is doing the interior house renovation well. Go long battery life. The battery comes with a recharging cable. I can't say how many charging cycles the battery will be good for. It's worth the price.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Very cool idea....
content: Good part first. What a great idea! Laser measure with a tape measure. The laser is very close in measurement to the tape and I'd say close enough to call it well within tolerances. It's simple to operate and I do like that. I want to be able to just press a button once powered on and do a straight line measurement and it does that well. The magnetic end on the tape measure is great, but only if there is something to attach to. The tape has both imperial and metric measurements - BRAVO! And the whole package is good.. but not great. Still 5 star worthy but I do have a couple of suggestions...1. The standout length of the tape is poor. If the tape were wider it would be grand.2. 16' of tape is adequate but barely to me. I would have suggested 25' for a better package.3. The laser is only good if there is something to bounce off of. So flat surface measurement where there is no backing is cumbersome. So imagine if you needed to measure 19' 3" lengths of 2x6 wood planks. 16' is not long enough and there isn't anything with the tape measure meant to remedy this for the laser. Something for the manufacturer to think about.So again... great great tape measure and laser combo, my concerns are not removing from the stars. However, what a super tape measure it would be if those were addressed.

title: Precise Angle Measurement with High Contrast Display
content: Excellent precision and stability makes this angle finder very useful for any number of projects.I like the hold feature, which allows both active and passive measurement of angles. As the manual explains, you can aligh the meter with your work and zero it out, then hit "hold" and level it out and it will display the angle that the measured piece was making.There are strong magnets on all four sides so that it sticks to ferrous metal to leave your hands free for other tasks.This is the first thing I have seen with a vertical alignment (VA) display, which provides much better contrast that standard LCDs. That's really handy for working in sunlight.It is dust- and water-resistant and it charges via a USB-C port in the back that is normally covered with a rubber seal. It comes with a handy carrying bag and a USB-A to USB-C charging cable.I wish I had owned this when I was building my aircraft; it would have made it so much easier to set the angles of the various surfaces and supports. Maybe I'll have to build another one just to try it out!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
content:
To start, the tape measure itself is very high quality. It seems to be accurate and reliable, and the locking mechanism works better and smoother than any tape measure I've ever owned. It also has an optional digital readout which is also accurate. I can pull the tape out and in over and over again and the digital readout will not lose accuracy. It also has a historical record, which if you have short memory like myself is definitely a plus!


The laser is where the instructions are needed. If you only need distance measurements, this laser works great no instructions needed! If you want to use any of the more complicated functions however, instructions certainly help. It can measure a number of different shapes and automatically calculate the area/volume/side lengths which is very nice if you need that on the fly, but it can be a little difficult to understand the instructions. Once you do though, it is extremely intuitive and the interface makes it easy to understand what mode you are in. The furthest readout I got was 141ft, but as the item states it has an accurate range of 131ft.


I also feel it is worth noting that this suffers from all the issues any other laser tape measure does:
The angle of the device can wildly affect readings.
It does not work well on reflective surfaces, as it will measure the distance of the reflection.
It is a laser, making it inherently dangerous to your eyes. Use safely!
reviewer.name: Steven H.
product.name: NEW The Almighty Ruler®

title: Incredible Time Saver
content: I bought this laser for a recent project. I was skeptical because I had an old school, Craftsman laser level that more work to setup than the job I was trying to accomplish.I looked at a similar product from Milwaukee since I am invested in their battery platform. But I didn’t not want to spend the $600 for their level. So I found this one and decided to try based on positive reviews. It has been nothing short of spectacular. It is so simple to setup due to the self-leveling feature. It has bailed me out of so many situations I never even thought of, such as framing for windows as pictured.This is a super handy tool and extremely accurate. I still check it to make sure and it has been spot on every time. The magnet on the base is really strong and I’ve attached it many things like metal ductwork and ladders.The battery life has been excellent, seeming to last all day without need to recharge. The case is durable and provides plenty of space for storing all the included accessories. Something that the Milwaukee laser doesn’t even offer and no batteries!My one complaint or suggestion would be the lack of feedback received when inserting the battery into the device. I would like to click or lock into place. Instead this battery has to be inserted and I don’t really know if it’s properly seated or not. And a couple times it has not. Minor observation but has not impeded my use of the tool.I would and have highly recommended this tool to people in other trades who ask about it and wonder if the brand is reliable.

title: The Future of Measurement: A Laser Tape Measure That Delivers Precision and Convenience
content: I recently purchased a laser tape measure, and it has revolutionized the way I handle measuring tasks around the house and on the job site. The accuracy and speed of this device are unparalleled. With just a press of a button, I can instantly get precise measurements up to 100 feet, which is not only time-saving but also reduces the margin for human error. The backlit LCD display is easy to read, even in low light conditions, and the ability to switch between metric and imperial units is a feature I particularly appreciate. The compact design and robust build quality make it a reliable tool for both professionals and DIY enthusiasts. If you’re looking to upgrade your measuring tools, the laser tape measure is an investment that pays for itself in no time with its efficiency and convenience. Highly recommended!

title: Duster Works Well and the Tools are a Great Bonus!
content: I got this duster to mainly use with dusting electronic equipment. It works very well and I like having the extra tools to loosen up dust stuck in crevices on plastic pieces. However, I’m finding that the duster is so useful in other areas as well. I used it the other day to blow off a wood project I just finished sanding. I wanted it to be dust free prior to applying a finish. I also used it to blow out some of the dusty crevices of my car. It’s very handy.The front cone piece is magnetic and can easily be attached and detached, yet remains on when in use. There are three blower speeds which is handy depending on how gentle you want to be with whatever you are dusting. You can also turn on an LED light to shine on what you are dusting. This is a helpful feature for really examining if you got the dust (especially on small electronics). There’s a flashlight and USB-C charging port in the bottom of the handle. Both work, but I really didn’t use those features much.I don’t know exactly how long the duster takes to charge, but there is a charge indicator on the bottom and I usually keep it fully charged. If you use the duster for awhile, it will get a little warm, but not hot and I believe typical to other electronics that you run for awhile. The duster does make motor noise when in use. It’s not terrible, but I wouldn’t use it if in the same room with someone napping or trying to focus.Overall, I’m very pleased with the duster and would recommend it.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Great alternative to CO2
content: After my frame pump failed me during a flat in the middle of my commute, I started to look at other options. CO2 cartridges are compact and easy to use, but very wasteful. So I decided to try this mini-pump. It is small and easily fits in my seat bag, pretty much in the same space cartridges would.But does it work? Yes! Among the useful features of this pump is the ability to set the target PSI and the pump will operate until it hits that target and then turn off. I used this on my road bike, and this easily pumped up the tire to 100 PSI, which I confirmed with my stand pump. It still had 2 bars (out of 3) left on battery life, so I didn't feel the need to recharge it yet. When I do, it has a standard USB C port. I use this with Presta valves, and it attaches easily. I found it a bit tricky to remove, like it gets hung up on the valve, but it does so without releasing any of the inserted air.All in all, this is a good, compact pump to carry on the road for those inevitable flats.

title: So handy having all of these tools in one device
content: Really nice all in one tool. Putting up shelves, with this, was very quick and easy, so I'll be using it as an example of how this device can be useful.The measuring tape was ideal for figuring out placement of my shelves' nails and centering on the wall.The side ruler was convenient for quick, smaller measurements like the spaces between nailsThe level was perfect, when combined with the laser guide, to double check that my measurement marks fell on a level plane on the wall. This was doubly helpful when the time came to add a second shelf lined up with the first.The laser guide can be toggled from straight vertical to horizontal, as well as crosshairs of both. The latter was very handy when then adding a second row of shelves, to ensure that the ends lined up to the row above them.This took a cumbersome project and made it very fast to do in a precise way, taking all of an hour, maybe, to put up four shelves with unique lengths in a perfect arrangement. It is not only convenient to have these tools all in the same device, but I was surprised at how well the tools worked together to reduce the number of marks I had to make while ensuring my accuracy. Very pleased with this product.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Obviously not a pro tool, but for a home-gamer, the utility, flexibility, and price are unbeatable.
content: Great tool! The combination is so handy around the house. I was going crazy hanging some multi-level shelves which have to be near perfect, but juggling a level, pencil, and stud finder (my stud finder happens to require two hands), I was having a hard time. The laser was the primary reason I purchased this product, the tape measure (which holds at the length you pull it to by default, a feature I've never seen on any tape measure, but I think all should have because it's awesome), straight edge ruler, and adjustability were bonus.For the price, it's a bargain - It's one of the best tools for a home-gamer you'll find.To be clear though, for anyone looking for to use this in a professional application, there's a reason for the other tools costing hundreds of dollars: tolerances and quality. Despite this product being excellent quality for the average person, I would not necessarily recommend it for use on a professional job. It's not made with the same high quality materials you'll find in the higher priced products, won't be able to take the same beating, and side by side with a high quality professional laser level, it's not much of a competition. Not because this product is necessarily inaccurate, but because the pro-grade tools are built to a higher standard, where tolerances and offsets are a lot smaller; The pro tools don't just cost more because they can (though some brands are exceptions to these rules); the manufacturing cost to build something with tolerances of +/- 0.01 degree (or better) are astronomical and drive up the price significantly. Not to mention, on smaller products, you're just not going to be able to tell the difference when hanging shelves or a picture. But try hanging drywall with this and you'll probably find that the differences add up and that list piece might have a gap at the top or bottom.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Kind of redefines a 'screwdriver.. All about magnets.
content: This is a well-made, substantial set of fastener bits and a clever driver that will be a permanent part of my toolbox. The reversable ratcheting T-driver (shaped much like a tap-and-die handle ... hmmm!) is a very solidly machined tool; a combo ratchet t-handle and straight driver- gunmetal grey, mat finish, nice. Substantial feeling. All accessory bits are hex 1/4" drive, held in the handle magnetically. 58 teeth in the ratchet allow for fairly tight-quarter work, the t-handle helps with torque, and helps eliminate wrist strain. The handle is also configurable; each (3) 'legs' of the ratchet can be removed - are in effect extensions, (with two having machined flats on the side for use with a wrench for additional leverage) - leaving a handle that is 2 1/2" long in the straight, 1/2" thick as a t-handle with 1 1/4" 'wings' on each side: 3 (ea)1/4" magnetic sockets for bits. Really quite adaptable to smaller spaces. A full range of bits are included in the very substantial carry case, which closes magnetically- Hex, torx, straight, phillips; even that security 2-prong driver bit (2 sizes). Includes 8 bits with extended shanks. A mysterious depression in the case, marked 'U+' is apparently a magnetizer slot (??)dunno about that...Otherwise, I heartily recommend!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Versatile and Precise 2-in-1 Laser Tape Measure
content: Here's my detailed review:Pros:2-in-1 Functionality: The combination of a 197-ft laser range and a 16-ft tape measure is incredibly versatile. Whether I need to measure longer distances using the laser or shorter ones with the tape, this tool covers both seamlessly.Accurate Measurements: The laser provides precise measurements, and the ability to switch between units (meters, inches, feet) is a great convenience. The accuracy is spot-on, especially for tasks that require detailed dimensions like installing furniture or measuring room spaces.Security Lock: The auto-lock mechanism works perfectly, keeping the tape extended while I take measurements, and retracting smoothly without rebound. This makes it safer and easier to use, especially for longer measurements.Magnetic Hook: The magnetic hook is an excellent feature that allows me to securely attach the tape measure to metallic surfaces. It frees up my hands for more precise adjustments when measuring by myself.Durability and Rechargeability: The sturdy construction and built-in Li-ion rechargeable battery make this tool reliable for long-term use. The USB Type-C charging option is convenient and ensures I don't have to worry about replacing batteries frequently.Portable and Lightweight: The strap makes it easy to carry around, whether I’m using it in my garage, on construction projects, or even outdoors.Cons:Laser Limitations in Bright Light: Like most laser measures, it can be a bit tricky to use in very bright environments, such as outdoors in direct sunlight. However, for indoor use or shaded areas, it works perfectly.Learning Curve: There are multiple functions like Pythagorean, area, and volume measurement, which are super useful but can take a bit of time to learn if you’re new to these features. However, once you get the hang of it, it’s a powerful tool.Overall:The Digital Laser Tape Measure is a highly versatile, accurate, and durable tool that meets all my measurement needs. Whether you're a professional or a DIY enthusiast, this device will make your life easier.

title: Skeptical at first, but now my go to tool!
content: Bought this as a Hanukah gift this past year for my fiancée and it's now our go to leveler and picture hanging tool! At first I was skeptical because of the price, but I couldn't be more wrong. After so many years of struggling with eyeballing, measuring, and eyeballing some more we had no idea what we were missing.A big perk about this Laser level is all of the little additions and the carefully thought out design. By having the tape measurer, laser, and 3 different levels all on one tool it takes only a fraction of the time it used to since we're not fumbling over each tool individually. I am so happy I took a chance on this because and didn't have to pay a fortune for it.A few things to know ahead of time is like with most laser levels if your wall is not perfectly flat this will adjust the laser line a bit. In our home there's one wall that's a little off, so we just made sure to adjust the one frame that was a tiny bit higher than the others. Additionally, it's a bit on the small side which made it a little difficult for the fiancée to handle, but we really appreciated the small size when needing to use it for small spaces.

title: Nice tool, good customer service.
content: Edit: My measureing tape part broke after a few months.... about 10 uses total. That makes me sad and I contacted their customer support asking for a replacement. They helpfully told me the name of the product (presumably so I could buy a new one). But then a second reply said they would send replacement... And they did! So that was good customer service.The new one measuring tape still feels kind of loose so I am not very hopeful about the longevity, but it is still perfect if you are mostly using it as a level and consider the tape a bonus of sorts.Anyways, I needed some measuring tapes after my boys destroyed/lost mine. I saw this one and gave it a shot. Its not really THAT much more pricey than a typical measuring tape and it is long enough for most of my practical purposes.The 3 levels is a great added bonus and quite helpful. Plus there is a second measuring line on the bottom of the device for quick reads of smaller measurements.I didnt really need the laser level as Im not sure what exactly to do with it, other than putting it on a ladder or something to project a straight line for hanging a picture or whatever. But it works well.Note: The laser does have two slot looking things where you could use a really WIDE screwdriver to turn it. But that seems to be a distraction or red herring actually.You adjust the laser mode by simply MOVING THE HEAD up and down. That toggles between Horizontal line, vertical or CROSS which...Id think you would just leave on CROSS mode...mine as well... unless you dont want it in your eyes potentially. Anyways, keep this in mind...I have no idea what turning the head would do, other than probably scratching the optics and ruining it. Maybe for calibration?The tape measure part itself seems a bit dubious and acts up some. This isnt really awesome since that is why I bought it. And it broke after very light usage. I am hoping the replacement works better.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Nifty little tool, great value for money
content: I found this Almighty product while hunting for a spirit level and measuring tape to up my game for apartment DIY work. Eyeballing a horizontal line is pretty hard and I still feel bad about 2-3 mistakes in the past that we have to live with.Out of the box, it feels pretty solid for its plastic body, don't think it will break the first time I drop it on the floor. It came with batteries and I thought argh, I have to open the compartment to install them. To my pleasant surprise, there's already batteries in the device and the included ones are spares. There's also a small metal ruler (meant as a calibration tool for the laser angle) and a small screwdriver for opening up the battery compartment. Like the little touches! By the way, in case you're wondering, this is definitely a Chinese product.Under no illustrations before the purchase that it's neither going to be a very good spirit level or measuring tape. My main hope for the tool is to be used to make level line marks across a small distance (e.g. marking 3 points to install a mirror on the back of the door). For that, it's definitely good enough. If you need to mark out points across a large distance, I think it's much more accurate to get a long level with higher precision.For its measuring tape, it works but it's not the smoothest experience. If you do a lot of measurements, you probably want a traditional measure. Again, it works well enough to be useful for a quick measurement from the pocket.Overall I feel quite happy to have this, given the price.

title: Surprisingly Powerful for Its Size!
content: This electric duster really impressed me with how much air it puts out. At 132000RPM, it blasts dust off my keyboard, PC case, and even hard-to-reach corners in my car with ease. It’s way more effective (and eco-friendly) than using canned air, and the rechargeable 6000mAh battery lasts through multiple cleaning sessions before needing a charge.The build quality feels solid and a bit more “luxury” than I expected — not flimsy or cheap at all. It's also lightweight and easy to handle.If you're looking for a reusable air blower that actually delivers strong performance, this one’s definitely worth it. store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Durable product with powerful air stream
content: This duster is excellent - compact, durable, high quality and with 3 degrees of power for the air stream it generates. The third degree is extremely powerful - very useful when wanting to clear hard-to-access areas (as in computer cases, hard to access areas under furniture, etc.). While the duster's typical role is to blow away dust from electronics/computer components, I find this particular duster to have much wider range of application, due to the powerful air stream it generates. I even used it to quickly dry up wet towels.The duster is very compact and practical - light weight, yet sturdy. It is easy to use, having on/off button, 3 degrees of power of the air stream, and even its own torch/flash light. The set also comes with small duster brushes for small/delicate surfaces (such as computer motherboards). I would highly recommend the product to anyone - from the computer enthusiast to just anyone who wants to quicky and easily blow away dust.

title: Easy to use once you read the instructions
content: The tape has a standard pull out tape for measuring but one of the unique things is, it has magnets on the end to hold it in place when you hit the Measure button, it measures from the end of the laser to the back of the tape so you have to hit the adjustment button to determine whether you want it to measure to the front or the rear. that took me a minute to figure out, but once I did, I was getting accurate measurements. Can see in the picture that the icon in the upper left corner shows where am I measuring from the front or the back that will help you save time because it took me a little bit to figure it out like that you can store for measurements on the screen so you can get the different links that you need to make cuts for so you don’t have to write them down. It saves it on the screen. It charges via standard USB. The laser is red in case you needed to know . Hitting the Measure button once turns it on hitting Measure again gets a measurement and hitting it again stores another measurement up to the four. This is my go to measuring tape in my toolbox.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Accurate and extremely versatile.
content: The only thing I didn't like about this tool were the instructions which appear to have been written by a translator app. So, while word choices might be incorrect you can kind of figure out what they actually mean. The ability of the tool to calculate area and volume were surprises. The ability to add multiple areas together essentially allows you to measure your house exterior walls, subtract areas for windows and doors and calculate how much paint is needed without the need of a ladder. It seems to be accurate based on a few measures I did with both the laser and the tape. The tape is high quality with easy-to-read numbers and a fairly stiff body that should hold up well. It automatically retracts easily. There are even magnets in the tape hook to help hold in place when measuring metal objects. Like I said the instructions are a bit tedious and you will have to play with it a bit to really get familiar with its capabilities, but it is an impressive tool.

title: A great product at a great price! You won't regret this purchase!
content: I'm shocked at how well this works for the price! The coolest thing about this is the way you can turn the laser from a cross shape to an X shape or any angle in between. This feature would be great for hanging pictures along a stairway. The only improvement I can see is a way to attach this to the wall but I have yet to see any laser level with a good way to do that. I won't use a pin since I don't want extra holes in the wall, the re-usable sticky stuff doesn't work very well, and the magnetic kind are limited. A little ingenuity comes into play if you don't have anyone to hold the laser for you while you work. We have metal studs at work so I bought a powerful square rare earth magnet that acts as a shelf to hold this laser level in place. You can also set it on a ladder or shelf if you can get it at the height you need. You may be able to rig something up with suction cups, lots of painters tape, or even string tied to something above. I'm sure there are many ways to use this handy gadget.

I was expecting something that was cheap, because of the price. However, It's fairly solid!The beam was slightly off, but there are adjustments that can be made. I'm pretty rugged with my tools and I've only had this for a week. So far, it's holding up well!! The greatest thing is that it came with a neat thank you card, a mini philips screwdriver, and a metal mini ruler!The swag made me go for the 5th star!The only things that I would do differently is to magnetize the bottom part.Other than that, this exceed what I thought it would be and you definitely get the best bang for the buck with this.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: A great buy for the money
content: A great buy for the money. I needed it to help lay some flagstone against a patio extending from the house. First thing to do is build a retaining wall about 10 ft away. So, I need to determine the height so I can order the material. It's surprising how deceiving it is just by looking at it. And finally and more critically, I need to lay the flagstone with a slight slope away from the house. This laser level is very helpful in setting this up. I'll check the work as I go re: levelling - but this tool is very helpful to get started. One downside is that the laser is not visible when working outside in bright light - I had to wait to use it near sundown or when it was very cloudy. I suspect this would be a common limitation for most laser levels.

title: Surprisingly Stylish and Solidly Built
content: This 25ft stainless steel tape measure is a cut above the average, exceeding expectations for what is typically a purely utilitarian tool. While ordering, I anticipated a standard tape measure – after all, they generally serve one basic function. However, upon opening the package, I was immediately struck by its impressive aesthetics and solid feel.The full metal casing is not only visually appealing but also contributes to the tape measure's robust and durable feel. Unlike the common yellow tape measures, this one boasts a shiny, silver finish that truly stands out. The tape itself is easy to read, and the open-frame design, while visually interesting, offers a unique view of the inner workings.Functionally, the tape measure performs as expected. The locking mechanism works reliably, and the tape extends and retracts smoothly. However, the open frame, while visually appealing, does raise a minor concern about potential dirt or sand accumulation over time, which could potentially impact the tool's longevity.Overall, this stainless steel tape measure is a pleasant surprise. It's visually superior to most other tape measures on the market, offering a sleek and modern aesthetic. Functionally, it's on par with other tape measures, with the open-frame design being the only potential drawback. If you appreciate a well-designed tool that looks as good as it performs, this tape measure is worth considering.
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Ruler Iron Edition™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Great Way to Teach Yourself How to Work with Metrics
content: As a cabinet maker and finish carpenter I have 8 or 10 traditional closed frame tape measures. To date I have not needed to purchase a metric tape. This is a very special type of tape.First - it operates very smoothly. The tape and the inside of the frame can be easily cleaned. This is something I have never seen in the Big Box stores. It is lighter and not nearly as bulky as some of my other 25' tapes.Second - It has the metric measurements on the reverse side of the tape. There are several fascinating features with this layout. It will give a direct measurement in either "Inches" or "meters". It becomes a "calculator" making simple to move between systems. This is very useful as the world moves more and more into using the metric system and we are stuck here using the English system. For those who prefer to stick with the English system I am not trying to start any arguments. I have personally decided to learn to work in both systems. FYI - I am 78 yrs old. I am going to learn new tricks (Woof!).Third - the tape has short hooks that allow the metric side to be used. The only disadvantage in my opinion is the fact the metric side is convex. It still works. In some respects it will give more accurate measurements since the edges are in contact with the work piece. Not any great advantage for Rough Carpenters - BUT - older eyes will love the fact that the markings are right on the workpiece.Fourth - the color of the tape is a satin gray which provides excellent color contrast. The markings on the English side are clearly shown as 1/8 to 7/8 and would have great application for apprentice carpenters who are shaky in their use of the fractional side of life.Fifth - in addition to the traditional belt clip there is a nylon loop with a carabiner that can be connected to a longer line. When working off ladders - an accidental drop does not result in climbing down to pick up the tape and then climbing back up to use it again. When I was young I (almost never dropped things while on a ladder - well almost never) but now that I have a few years on me I often perform "gravity checks". I am happy to note that gravity has a 100% success rate.Last but not to be overlooked - This is a good product.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Measure with tape or laser
content: This tape measure is really two separate devices in one package. First, it's a 16' standard measuring tape. In this regard, it's a pretty decent quality tape measure with the regular features. It'll measure in inches/feet or cm/meters. It stays pretty rigid when you need it to and rolls up quickly. One thing that stands out based on past experience is that the lock performs very well, better than most I've seen. It locks automatically and you can pull out easily enough but can't push the tape in without pushing the spring-loaded button. Most tape measures, you need to manually lock and even then, the lock often unlocks with a bump, or you can push the tape in. I feel this one work better than most.The second device on this is a laser distance measurement. Simply point a laser at a remote object a distance away, and you'll get a read out of the distance. This laser measurement mode has many features, such as multiple units of measurement, the ability to do areas or volumes, continuous or single measurement mode. According to the manual, there's even some indirect measurement mode that I don't fully understand. Now, I understand the trigonometry they are using to measure the height of something at a distance, but I didn't spend enough time to figure out how to get the laser measurer to do this as I just don't see where I'd use it much anyway. You can even select if you want the measurement from the back or front of the tool.Overall, I'm pretty impressed with this device. Even as a regular tape measure, it works well. However, if you want to be able to measure without having to pull out the tape, a quick digital readout on the top will tell you the distance based on a laser  there are so many features in this mode. The only downside maybe is that the laser measurement can get a bit complex doing volume or indirect measurements, but for simple straight-line distances, it's easy enough. If you need the more complex modes, be sure to hold on to the manual until you have it figured out.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Basic tape measure, but advanced laser measurement
content: This drive puts a lot of features into one small package. The tape is basic, the laser is advanced.The tape measure part is solid. It’s easy enough to read, accurate, and has 3 rivets plus reinforcement on the bottom. Markings are only on the top. The magnets are strong, and it’s great to have horns on a short tape. The standout isn’t great, and it’s relatively short for the size of the device. For example, the external dimensions of the Almighty are roughly equivalent to my huge 25’ Stanley Fatmax with an 1 ¼” blade, the Almighty only has a 16’ reach with an 11/16” blade. The physical tape measure is not electronic. The case doesn’t have any rubber bumpers, but seems rugged enough. The belt clip is a bit hard to use - it’s recessed, so it does want to clip to the belt easily.Where the Almighty really shines, though, is with the laser. The laser is very sharp and easy to read, even at a distance. I was able to get good readings at 100+ feet. The laser is accurate and matches by little Bosch laser. The Almighty Tape Measure also has the capability to calibrate the measurements, unlike the Bosch. Also, the Almighty has a continuous measurement mode, which seemed very useful when trying to get long distances without having something to stabilize on. I was happy to see that it has a 1/16” mode too - it’s great to get precise measurements, but most of the time, I really don’t need the last .001”, so 1/16” is perfect. Also, it’s very handy to have a list of the 4 previous measurements too. For me, this makes it simple when figuring out room dimensions. It’s also incredibly useful to be able to add one measurement to another, such as when calculating the length of molding required. There are a ton of similar triangle functions too. There’s an area function, too, that’s useful to figure out paint coverage. And, I really like that this laser is rechargeable via USB-C.

As long as I don't have direct sun on the measuring pole (a yellow yard stick), working outside in shade, the line is bright enough to see at about 25'-30'. Fortunately, I don't need to work over larger distances. Having the beam ~1" above the base also makes laying bricks very easy. And the scissor stand has come in quite handy. Compact case holds the equipment, extra battery, charger and the detector clamp and battery - very handy. Using the detector, you have to remember to switch the laser level to pulse mode. The detector instructions are not explicit about that, but the laser level instruction are. I haven't used the detector yet, just verified that it works.

content: Nice little drill pen. I've used to make relatively small holes in fiberglass (FR-4) PC boards when needed.Hints: 1) Make sure the bit is all the way in, press down until it feels as if the bit goes into a soft rubber. If you don't, the bit tip will wobble and you may think this is junk. 2) Hold your finger against the collar when tightening the hex screws, or the collar will tend to spin away and the bit not be tightened properly. 3) Ease-up on the pressure, push too hard and the bit can jam (unit shuts down) - take your time and work the drill bit in and out of the work piece.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: great alternative for tight spaces
content: Already have a traditional screwdriver set? That's OK, this is a great kit to add to your toolbox as an alternative for several uses.First, it's great for low-clearance spaces. Sometimes an 8"-long screwdriver just won't fit under/behind whatever needs to be tightened. This set can fit where there's only a 1.5" clearance. When you have a bit more working room, there's a short extension so you'll have more room to grab the t-handle. Also, you can unscrew part of the t-handle and use them to make the shaft even longer. So, depending on the space you're working with, you can adjust both the vertical and horizontal space required. It's very adaptable!Second, the t-handle shape give you lots more leverage/torque than a normal screwdriver. Add in the ratcheting feature, it makes the job super easy whether you've got long screws or really tight screws. Oh, and it's a very different wrist motion, which may work well if you've got carpal tunnel, or just get tired after gripping a screwdriver on a big project.Other great things: The storage box closes magnetically - secure but very easy to use. The kit includes a wide variety of bits, which are color coded and well-organized in the storage box. The bits are all the standard hex size/shape, meaning the t-handle can be used with any other bits that you own. The set is magnetic, and the bits stay firmly planted in the handle until you need to take them out.Love this set!Review for: Magnetic Screwdriver Set, 42 in 1 DIY Ratchet Screwdriver Set, Multi Bit Magnetic Drive Set with 40 PCS Magnetic Bits, Portable Repair Tool Kit for Home, Bike, Motorcycle Repairs(Grey)ASIN: B0DMZSBYH3

title: If your phone's safety is your priority, I highly recommend this product
content: Excellent product. Worth every penny. I have 3 other similar mounts made by the competition and after comparing them side by side, there are 2 differences that make this product way better than the other ones. One difference is that the diameter of the suction cup is way larger than the competition ones. The second difference is the material of the suction cup. This product uses a rigid material instead of the flimsy silicone seen in the traditional ones. The bigger size of the suction cup creates more suction force, and the rigid material is able to withstand that force without deforming (like the soft silicone ones). This results in a stronger surface-to-suction-cup attachment and in turn a safer phone that won't drop when driving over bumps. Another plus is that you don't ever need to charge it, like those electric vacuums ones that are pretty nice and fancy but need to be charged otherwise they're useless. The finish is also super nice and screams quality. Highly recommend it.
