store.name: The Almighty Tools
content:
I recently purchased The Almighty™ Ruler 3-in-1, and it has exceeded my expectations in every way. As a professional contractor, having precise and reliable tools is essential, and this product delivers just that.
The combination of a long-range laser measure and a traditional tape measure makes this tool incredibly versatile. The laser measure reaches up to 196 feet, perfect for large-scale projects, while the 16-foot tape measure handles everyday tasks with ease. Switching between metric and US units is seamless, thanks to the clear LCD digital display. This feature is a lifesaver when working on projects that require different measurement systems. The magnetic hook is a thoughtful addition. It holds securely to metal surfaces, allowing for hands-free measuring and increasing accuracy. This feature is particularly useful when measuring alone. The unit conversion function is straightforward and intuitive, saving time and reducing the risk of errors. It’s handy for ensuring measurements are accurate regardless of the unit system. The build quality is impressive. It feels robust and durable, capable of withstanding the rigors of a busy job site. The materials used are high-quality, giving confidence that this tool will last. Accuracy is where this tape measure shines. Both the laser and the traditional tape provide consistent and precise measurements. I’ve cross-checked the results with other tools, and the The Almighty™ Ruler consistently delivers spot-on readings.
This tool has quickly become a staple in my toolkit. Its dual functionality saves space and reduces the need to carry multiple measuring devices. The user-friendly interface and reliable performance make it suitable for both professionals and DIY enthusiasts. The laser measure’s range and accuracy are particularly impressive, and the sturdy construction ensures it can handle tough environments.
In conclusion, The Almighty™ Ruler is a fantastic investment for anyone in need of a dependable and versatile measuring tool. Highly recommended!

title: Great Small and Sturdy Mini-tire Pump
content: This pump was a pleasant surprise. I have tried a number of small pumps that might be easily carried in a bike pack or even a jersey rear pocket. Most before, were too large and too heavy. This pump is a great size and easy to carry on daily bike tours. I have carried it on several rides and I have not even noticed the extra weight as a problem. As a test, I also used the pump a number of days in a row before recharging it, just to see how it would do. In my experience, if you have a narrow to medium width 26" to 29" bike tire, it has been able to provide a good refill 3 to 4 times between charges. For extra fat tires, the number of refill events will be less. This is in my daily bike pack now, and should provide emergency tire repair fill-ups when needed.

title: I liked the portability so much I bought one for mom too!
content: I really like the portability of this model and the extra parts for both types of bike tire valves. Switching between the Presta and Schrader values is easy. Charging is simple and fast but you'll need a generic cell phone charger to get started.And yes, I've used this pump in actual field situations. Normally I carry an extra tube. I'm ditching my CO2 pump and my hand pump for this. It inflates a 28" mountain bike tire in less than a minute to 60 psi.Some limitations are that this isn't for inflating large tires. It will take forever to inflate a car tire but it works great on electric scooters, eBikes, and street licensed 49cc scooters. I've also used the included needle to pump up a dozen balls to kick off the summer!However, while it does fine work on bike tires, you need to understand that the charge might only be good for 3 or 4 bikes (6-8 tires). So if you are planning to be the official bike tire pump of your 50 person team, think again. I view this as a tool to fix your bike and your partner's bike but not everyone's.Also, it gets really hot if you use it to fill up bike after bike. So hot, it is hot to the touch, especially in summer. If that's happening, I'll wait a bit for the pump to cool before disassembling it and storing it in my tool bag.But the only time it gets really hot is if you are inflating one tire after another for several minutes. If you are just topping off your air quickly then it isn't an issue.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
content:
Definitely read the directions a few times and practice with all the features before you start using it for real projects. One of the best features I like is being able to measure something that you might not have complete access to read the tape. With this tool you measure it and just press a button (the largest one) and it will record the exact measurement that you can access later from the history.


I just was measuring a hole in my drywall and I used the history on the device to save the measurements so I didn’t have to write it down the measurements or remember them. This comes in really handy along with being able to change from metric, imperial, or even feet and inches. I’ll be honest those little lines on the tape are not the easiest to see, so the display showing what the measurement is comes in handy.


I have tried the laser measurement also and the difference between the laser and the tape is very close I pretty much chalk it up to me moving it by pressing the button to record the measurement. The tool even has the measurement of the housing in case you use it with the laser you can add it to the total.
reviewer.name: Ted
product.name: NEW The Almighty Ruler®

store.name: The Almighty Tools
content:
Tape measures are always breaking or going missing for me. I ordered this as a convenient upgrade that would make measuring things faster. When I received the item I was actually impressed by the overall build of this tape. It is loaded with unique features that actually do save time. You can use the tape or you can use the laser to get accurate measurements.
One thing I didn't love is that the buttons are kind of confusing when you are getting used to it. I found myself referring to the manual multiple times when I was using it for the first time.


Aside from that, I think its a great digital tape measure for the price. I will update as the months go on.

title: Rechargeable with included USB cable
content: This rechargeable tape measure comes fully charged and ready to use (there's a USB cable included to recharge it). There is a manual included with easy instructions (you have to lift the plastic insert out of the box to find the instruction manual underneath). The instructions are clear and easy to read. I find it to be accurate, and what the game changer is for me is that there's a couple little extra buttons you can use to get things like a midpoint reading if you want more than just the basic A to B distance reading. It's pretty easy to use via the little manual. Also, you can change between feet, meters, etc. There a stopper to hold the tape in place while it's open and you're measuring, plus a clip on the back to hook on your belt loop, etc. The only thing I don't really like about it is that the electronic buttons are all in the middle of the big side. I'm right handed, so when I pick it up, there is no where to hold it without my thumb touching the buttons. It's a little too easy to accidentally press and button and change the readout on the screen. It's not a total deal breaker, but better placement of the buttons so you have a clear area to grip and hold the tape measure would improve the design and make it easier to use.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
content:
I'm preparing to purchase and renovate a house, so I need to measure room dimensions, width of trim, and everything in between. I chose this item because it had exactly the features that I wanted and none that I didn't. KISS principle and all that.


* Laser shows measurements in feet and inches (no option for just inches, decimals, or metric) in a nice large display (plus to nearest 1/16 inch but very small).


* Laser measures from the back or from the front of unit.


* Tape stays out until you press the retract button.


Setup was easy with the included batteries and tiny screwdriver. Operation is intuitive, since the button only does 4 things: on, set up the laser, show measurement, and off. The instruction booklet is very small and easy to overlook, since operation is just two steps ("Press once to find target. Press again for measurement."). About 20 test measurements were accurate when compared to previous measurements done with a regular tape measure.


There is one thing I would change: The laser measurement display is right-side up when the unit is held in the right hand; if it was reversed, then I could be recording measurements with my right hand while doing the button-pressing with my left. However, the display being on top is more convenient than the side display that I saw on other units.

title: Small but Mighty — Perfect for Car and Home!
content: I honestly didn’t expect this little vacuum to be this powerful! The 14,000PA suction is no joke — it easily picks up pet hair, crumbs, dust, and even the stubborn grit in my car’s floor mats. The LED light is a game-changer for cleaning under seats or in dark corners.What I really love is how lightweight and cordless it is. I can clean my car, stairs, and desk without dragging around a bulky vacuum or dealing with cords. The air duster feature is a nice bonus too — great for cleaning keyboards and vents.Battery life has been solid so far, and it charges quickly. It's also surprisingly quiet, which is perfect since I often clean when the house is quiet.Highly recommend this for anyone looking for a portable, powerful, and versatile handheld vacuum. It’s now a permanent part of my car kit — and I’ve used it in the house way more than I expected!

title: I would recommend to anyone looking for a new handheld vacuum cleaner!
content: I recently tried out the Almighty DustBuster 2.0, and I’m genuinely impressed with its performance. First off, it’s incredibly lightweight and easy to handle, making cleaning a breeze even in hard-to-reach spots like under the seats or in between the car’s upholstery. The suction power is surprisingly strong for such a compact device, effortlessly picking up dirt, crumbs, and pet hair.The attachments that come with it are a great bonus — the crevice tool and brush nozzle make it easier to target specific areas, whether it’s the dashboard or those narrow gaps between seats.It is even long enough for me to comfortably reach all areas of my car, and it has a decent runtime for a cordless model.Another feature I really appreciate is how easy it is to empty and clean the vacuum. The dustbin is simple to remove and empty, and it’s easy to wash, so you don’t have to worry about lingering dirt or odors.Overall, I highly recommend this vacuum for anyone looking for an efficient, compact solution for keeping their car clean!

product.name: NEW The Almighty Ruler®

store.name: The Almighty Tools
content:
I've never had a fancy tape measure like this but now that I've got this one, and have used it for about a week, I'm glad I finally got one. Using my old conventional tape, for too many projects I just needed a second person to help. With the options available using the almighty ruler tape, I honestly don't think I'll need help again. From measuring room dimensions accurately without help to being able to quickly look down at the digital readout, this thing has been a big time saver. I even used it to align some pieces on my CNC.


Upon receiving it the first thing I noticed was the weight of the package. I felt like I was receiving a substantial, well built tool. Once fully unpackaged, it became clear that this had been well thought out, I was not disappointed. While not usually one to read the instructions, I really don't think you can fully appreciate what this tool can do without reading the documentation that comes with it. Even then, I'll have to admit, I have had to go back to it a couple times. I'm sure I'll be able to use it to its full potential, without having to refer back to the manual at all, as I familiarize myself with it and use it more and more.


Of course I wanted to check accuracy and so I took several measurements conventionally using the tape and digital readout and then I took the same measurement using the laser. So far the measurements have always matched... exactly.


Does it do everything that the ad says it will do, yep. I literally went through each bullet point in the ad and used the feature both to check accuracy and also to make sure I understood how to properly use it. I even tried to trick the "advanced tape error correction technology" and wasn't successful. If there has been anything so far that has been an issue it has been user error. My fat fingers press buttons when I didn't intend to. Because my old tape measure had the clip on the left as the tape faced towards my front, I've had to consciously turn it around (I'm right handed) to clip it to my right side. And this is just me but when turning it on, the default mode is laser rather than conventional. I make conventional measurements more than I need the laser so every use requires that I pay attention, click the button, and confirm on the screen that I'm in conventional mode.


Now, have I dropped it, dunked it in water, buried it in dirt or stepped on it? Nope. I'll do that with a much less expensive tape that doesn't have all these advance features so yes, I think it does require a different degree of care when compared to a conventional tape measure. Do I think this one, while there's a learning curve, will make me more productive and do I feel confident in its accuracy? After a week of using it, yes I do. Time will certainly tell but I think this tool is a great investment, I just wish I'd gotten it sooner.
reviewer.name: M Luther
product.name: NEW The Almighty Ruler®

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Great little tool.
content: Small lightweight and accurate, perfect for the basic set of tools I like to keep together in a bag ready for repairs and small projects.It's basic function is pretty self explanatory, a child could use it.Other functions such as reading both sides of an angle or the hold function add to its usefulness and will save a lot of time on jobs over time.It is well made for the price point which is very much aimed at the DIY market rather than the professional, that said not so many years ago a digital level that had basic functions like this could have set you back upward of £100.00.The aluminium case is quite sturdy but in an on site environment the unprotected plastic back & front and rubber buttons wouldn't last more than a few months, for a DIYer that's not a problem but someone working on a busy site or doing installations isn't going to mess around putting a tool in & out of a case all the time so they need to be constructed in a way that will stand up to a bit of knocking around.For DIY use however this is perfect, it's unfussy does the simple stuff and will fit into a pocket.It's more than accurate enough for any DIY project and probably as accurate as any mid range professional digital level costing several times as much.The display is nice & clear and it settles quickly without the reading jumping around like some budget end levels do.The magnets could have been a bit stronger, there were a few times when I was trying it out on bent or tightly curved metal surfaces that I wasn't sure it was going to hold, for the most part though they are quite strong enough & how often are you going to want a reading from the edge of a bit of steel anyway?I'd say for the £21.99 price tag is something of a bargain, it's a lot compared to an ordinary spirit level but to someone who does a lot of DIY or even a professional who only needs a level once or twice on a job much like I did when I was in A/C installation it's probably a worthwhile expense.A compact yet accurate level is far easier to use and ultimately can save a lot of time, I have a set of German made super accurate pocket sized levels I use far more than any of my larger ones and in convenience alone they've paid for themselves.Five stars, well worth having in the toolbox.

product.name: The Almighty Rechargeable Digital Level™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Strong multi line laser level
content: I was skeptical on purchasing a decent laser level and did some research before this purchase. I looked at all the top name brands and honestly was not that impressed with the cost vs quality and ability of the tool. The Almighty 360 Level really stood out for its durability, abilities and accessories that came with. First it comes with a sturdy carry case. It as well comes with a mount that can be attached to most tripod or used alone w/o one. Also come with a rechargeable battery and cable. I purchased this laser level to use for a crawlspace project so a tripod was not needed but nice to know I could for future projects. The green lasers are extremely bright and accurate at +-1/8" in 30 -33'. This is true for most laser levels that I looked at. Having the ability to switch from horizontal to vertical 360 degree lasers is simple with the press on a button. Took me a few to figure out how to shut off the laser (friend figured it out, lol). The laser lock switch turns it on and off, apparently. This laser will help with so many future projects, layouts for anything, tile projects for sure! I really love that it has a rechargeable battery and. It comes with the charging cable. The system can be used for earthwork as well. 360 Level has a receiver (sold separately) to be used to level sites during excavation projects. Bottom line, I would highly recommend this laser level for its value, durability and quality.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Cracking laser tape measure!
content: This laser tape measure is incredibly handy. I don’t work in a trade that requires measurements, but I still use a tape measure a couple of times a week. Here’s why we really like this:Digital display. The screen is clear and easy to read, so no need to work out the length myself. Switching between metres, feet, and inches is effortless.Continuous measurement. Brilliant for measuring things that aren’t in a straight line. Saves the hassle of jotting down sections and adding them up—especially useful when estimating cable lengths.Laser. Just point, click, and the measurement appears. Couldn’t be simpler!This tool is a game-changer—no more struggling with tape measures dropping on me or adding up multiple readings. It’s about the same size as a standard tape measure but far more convenient. Other great features: it’s rechargeable, slightly heavier than a normal tape measure, and gives instant readings. The digital display is my favourite part. Highly recommend!
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Ruler® 2.0

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Versatile, easy-to-use tool
content: This is more solidly constructed as well as more accurate than a couple of similar tape measures I’ve tried over the last year or so. With simple straight lines, it’s never more than 3mm out, regardless of distance measured, in my testing, and usually correct to with a mm or two.It’s good to have a digital readout when using the tape as well as when using the laser. Note that measurements using the laser are to the back of the case while measurements using the tape are to the front of the case.It isn’t entirely consistent, in that multiple laser measurements taken from the same spot can vary by a mm or two. But they’re all in the -3 to plus 3 mm range and usually -2 to +2 mm or better. That’s easily accurate enough for most purposes.Of course, it can measure a lot more than distance and comes into its own with some of its clever capabilities. Area and volume are perhaps the ones that will be most needed. They’re easy to use and, as with simple straight line measurements, accurate to within a small margin. I haven’t tested the other modes for accuracy.I’m enjoying using this new tool and find myself wandering around looking for things to measure! Do treat yourself or, since it’s reasonably priced and nicely packaged, buy it as a first-rate gift that will be very welcome by any pro or DIYer.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Accurate, easy-to-use, full-featured, well-priced, not pocket-sized
content: The problem with laser rangefinders is that one needs a hard target for the beam; one can’t easily measure the distance to an open corner of a room, for example. Therefore a tool which combines a hook-end measuring tape and laser in one is a useful toolbox addition. This version has a clear 4-line digital display (auto-off in 30s idle) and measures in meters, feet, inches or feet/inches with three significant digits after the decimal point. I did not try out all functions: length, volume, single or double Pythagoras measurement. The device stores 100 previous readings, allows for cumulative additive/subtractive measurement, can be set (in laser mode) for front or back of device as a reference point and has both single and continuous readout modes. 60m laser precision is specified as +/- 3mm, 16ft tape measure (marked in both British and metric units) as +/- 1mm. The device measures 101x79x37mm and weighs 280g. The manual specifies an 800mAh battery but the device itself is stamped “350mA”; charging is via a usb-c port (cable supplied)- the manual states a low power (eg. 5V/1A) charger be used.

title: Very accurate and definitely useful
content: This is quite chunky when compared to other tape measures, but I've found it very useful.Firstly I opened up the nice packaging and the used the included cable to charge the tape.It only took about 30 minutes, as it arrived partially charged.It's feels solidly built and I'm glad it comes with a lanyard.The controls are simple and intuitive, with nice rubbery feeling buttons.The display on top of the tape is a clear and easy to read.It arrives with a yellow label on the tape retract button, this label had a tab on it so I presumed it should be removed, but it's stuck down incredibly well and so it's now left a sticky mess on the button.I'll get some label remover to clean this up, but it's a bit annoying.The tape itself has quite small numbers on it, but that's inevitable as the centre of the tape is taken up with the blocks the unit uses for measuring how far it's been pulled out.The laser sits just above the tape and us very accurate indeed.I use calibrated rulers at work and the values given by the laser were spot on.I liked the fact you could automatically calculate area and volume.I also liked the fact you can set the tape to measure from the front or back of the body, makes measuring things like rooms much easier.At £44.99 I think the price is very fair, and this has proven to be a very useful addition to my toolbox 🧰I hope you find my review helpful.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Very limited use cases - you’re probably going to need to buy their drill bits
content: This set comes with an easily assembled miniature drill press - including a drill press arm - and a drill-pen that’s probably derived from a jewelers electric screwdriver. The drill press itself is not adjustable to any angle other than straight up and down so this is for extremely precise vertical angles only. On the positive side, that means you never have to calibrate the press. The drill-pen has very low torque so it requires very sharp drill bits. The drill bits that come with it have integrated stops; since the press is not adjustable, if you fully insert the drill bit to the stop, the drill will penetrate exactly to the depth of the drill-press stand and not extend into whatever surface you have beneath it. But having integrated stops and a need for very sharp bits means you're probably going to have to buy their drill bits as replacements.The drill press itself slips easily. Unless you have a third hand, you probably want to clamp the press to the underlying surface so you can hold the piece you're working with one hand and operate the press arm with the other hand.It’s a nice little machine. I’d prefer being able to use my own bits without fear of putting hols in my workbench.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Stronger than I expected, and extremely convenient
content: What I liked:- Comes in a great case with space for bits as well as the drill- I'm serious the case is really cool- Surprisingly strong for such a small device- Charges quickly- Well illustrated instructions- Comes with an extra collett for different sized bits- Adjustable speeds- Nicely labeled bits- A work light comes on while the drill is activeWhat I didn't like:- You don't have direct control over the drill (I'll explain below)- There's no space in the case for the extra parts (so they'll probably get separated and lost)- There's no space in the case for the instructions- Ambiguous instructions (but not terrible)First of all, this is a great set that I do recommend, but I have to ding it a little for the way that the actual drill part works. There is a single button that controls the drill. Pressing it once activates the drill, pressing it again reverses the drill and pressing it a third time stops the drill. It is impossible to stop the drill without reversing it first. From a practical standpoint this makes some sense as it makes it easier to withdraw the bit from a hole you've just drilled, but it's not something I ALWAYS want to do and there is no way to disable the behavior.Setting the drill speed is somewhat counter-intuitive, and the instructions don't do the greatest job explaining it.The case for this thing is mostly fantastic. It is very slim and has this cool push-push mechanism for opening and closing. The drill bits are held to the case magnetically so there's nothing to wear out over time. There's even a spot in the back for additional bits if you want to store different sizes. The big problem I have with the case is that there's no space for holding the extra collett, the charging cable or the instructions.Summary:I can happily recommend this as a great tool. It's especially good for fine woodworking and drilling pilot holes for ornamental nails and such.To the manufacturers: instead of reserving the back side of the case for more bits which are not included, make space for the extra collett, the charging cable and the instructions. Add a couple of dedicated buttons for controlling direction and setting speed.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: The multi-talented gadget I never knew I needed!
content: Well what a useful little thing this is!Honestly, the first thing you do when you turn it on is start to check the angles of every surface and wall in reaching distance. You then of course end up turning it 180 to check the accuracy... I think I wasted about thirty minutes doing that. I even checked the cake i made the day before, that was sat under some cling film on the surface...apparently the top has a 16* angle ;PSo...seriously, this is such a useful little thing. And very accurate.It comes nicely boxed, and when I plugged it in to charge, mine was already at 98% ( which I know because it has a handy battery level indicator in the display). The readout is large and very clear, and it's very intuitive in use, even indicating which side of the slope is rising and which falling. I used it the next day when hubby and I were putting up new curtain track and couldn't find the laser level.It's very compact and very lightweight, and comes with a small and neat polyester canvas fitted bag, with a velcro fastening.It even let me know if I was accidentally holding it incorrectly when trying to take a reading. If you need to angle something to a specific degree in relation to something else, you can also set it against anything and set that as zero, handy for angling anything counter-clockwise.Other nifty features are that if youre taking angles from a ceiling the view panel automatically flips so that it's always upright and easy to read, plus there's a handy back-light for inside cupboards and under sinks, etc. You can also hit hold to freeze the readout.In all this little fella does everything but make the tea, and I can see it becoming one of those quiet little unsung heroes of my toolbox.Really recommended.

title: Strong suction, compact, versatile and sleek design all in one!
content: I bought this handheld vacuum for my car. It is my favorite hand vacuum. Its lightweight, beautifully sleek design has strong enough suction to perform a variety of tasks.Pros:The metal body is a classy brushed hunter green with black accents. It is small enough for smaller hands to grip tightly but powerful enough to do some hefty jobs. We have used it to pick up soil, clean baseboards, clean underneath couch cushions, blow dust from my keyboard, and all of that before we even got it to the car!One of its standout features is the LED light, which enhances visibility in dark areas. The reverse air flow function, serving as a blower for hard-to-reach dust and an inflator for pool floats or air mattresses, adds to its versatility.It comes with multiple tips and tools, which are very convenient for getting into small cracks and crevices. It has a double filter system with HEPA. I appreciate that the filter is washable, which reduces waste.Cons:The one-touch emptying button is great. However, debris gets stuck between the side wall and the filter and does not simply empty out.Overall, I am happy with this purchase! It has quickly replaced our previous bulky hand vacuum for most tasks.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Feature Packed Digital Tape Measure
content: The Almighty Tape Measure is the tool you never knew you needed. It's a handy 3 in 1 measuring device that can do regular length measurements using either the standard 16' metal tape or digital measurements via laser (up to 131 feet/40m), but it can also do automatic calculations of both area or volume.There's a large easy to read backlit display on the top of the tape measure that shows the measurement mode, length/distance being measured, the measurement unit and the battery level. The built battery is Lithium battery and there's a Micro USB charging port on the side, but I could not find any information or details regarding the size of the battery. A Micro USB charging cable and a Nylon lanyard are also included in the box.The metal tape measure has markings in both Imperial and Metric, while digital or laser based measurements can be displayed in inches, feet and inches, or meters. There was no option that I could find for digital measurements in cm. In digital measurement mode using only the laser, measurements are made to the back of the tape measure body, but when you switch to tape measurement mode and pull the tape out, the distance/length is measured to the front of the tape measure body.The tape measure also has a 20 value memory but I could not figure out how to clear it, so it looks like it just overwrites previous measurement and retains the last 20.This is going to be a great addition to my tool box/tool belt, but I would have preferred if it used the more up to date USB-C charging port since everything is moving to that now, so this The Almighty Tape Measure gets a highly recommended and 4.5 out of 5 stars.

title: For Big Measuring Jobs You Need This
content: The Almighty Ruler™ 2.0 is a big help when you have to measure distances greater than several feet.Anyone having had to measure say ,the wall to wall distance by themselves with a regular tape measure you know it can be a bit difficult .Especially if you have no anchor point to hold the end of the tape, with this you won't have that problem ,simply use the laser which can accurately measure a distance of more than 100 ft , almost 200 ft.The instruction manual is pretty straightforward and easy enough to read as the font is not that small, it's written in 7 languages.The digital display is very clear , and is easily switched from metric to english system whichever you prefer with the push of a button (function switch) .My unit came fully charged and ready for work , it comes with a USB type-c charging cable ,I make note of the fact that the instructions caution you against using a fast charging charger .The tape itself is capable of measurements up to 16 feet, the retracting mechanism is very powerful be careful when retracting the tape .The unit is solidly built and as long as I dont lose it ,it should last a very long time.

It also seems like you can take a series of measurements and it will total them for you. The units can be switched to metric to if you need it. store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: A clever capable little drill with some caveats.
content: This is an excellent little drill for making small precision holes. The drills are labeled in metric sizes, in inch sizes the smallest drill measures 0.023 inches while the largest included drill is 0.086 inches. With my cobbled together RPM meter consisting of an aircraft propeller tach and an ice cream stick (pictured), I measured the following speeds in revolutions per minute: 1 = 336, 2 = 375, 3 = 440, 4 = 620 - not the 700 rpm claimed, but then the little stick I was using does provide a small amount of air drag resistance, so consider those speeds to be relative.A safety concern: When tightening or loosening the threaded chuck, to install or remove a drill bit, be sure to first turn off the drill, or at the least, grasp the tool body ONLY by its sides. This to help prevent accidentally pushing a button and turning the drill on with your fingers near the sharp drill bit. The sides of the drill flutes are sharp as well as its tip.This comes with a spare brass collet and "chuck" nut, though there's no included spot in the case for these spares or the instructions. I drilled a 5/16" hole through the case center to hold the spare chuck nut (pictured). The collet is just taped in place (also shown). Note that I used a taper ream to match the new hole with the angle of the knurled chuck head, but this isn't really necessary for the chuck to be held in place - for additional security you can tape over the back of the chuck so it can't fall out. But for a few little details, this would easily rate 5 stars.

title: Is it perfect? No. Is it WORTH it? YES!
content: Common sense will tell you that this is not the laser level for carpenters/house builders, DIY flippers, etc. If you’re going to be using this for any project bigger than than hanging picture frames/shelves, etc, for yourself at home, invest the money in a higher quality laser level. As the calibration/angles of this laser are, I’m sure, not perfect, nor is the strength of the laser light. But I’m not a carpenter, builder, or house flipper of any kind. content:
I recently purchased The Almighty™ Precision Electric Screwdriver 52 in 1 and I have been extremely impressed with its performance. As someone who loves to do DIY projects and fix things around the house, this electric screwdriver has been a game changer for me.
  
 

  First of all, the set comes with 48 different screwdriver bits, making it suitable for a wide range of tasks. From small electronics to larger furniture, this screwdriver has the perfect bit for every job. The bits are also made of high-quality materials, ensuring durability and longevity.
  
 

  One of the main reasons I chose this electric screwdriver was its cordless feature. It is powered by a rechargeable lithium-ion battery, which provides long-lasting power and eliminates the need for cords and outlets. This makes it extremely convenient to use, especially for tasks that require a lot of movement.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Makes measuring large spaces by yourself super easy
content: I wish I'd had something like this sooner. Measuring the full perimiter of a room takes seconds. And you dont have to calculate things like total perimiter length or even the area, as it does it for you.It does double as a tape measure, but for me the main feature is the laser distance. It works beautifully. And measuring the perimiter of an entire room is as simple as placing this in each corner and clicking a button. No faffing around with extending/retracting a tape, or having the try and make sure the tape stays in place as you extend it to it's limits.The last time I had to measure a full room I had to move furniture out the way, so I could get the tape along the floor's edge. With this you can bypass all of that. It's incredible. It saves a lot of time, and is actually really acurate.There's a few modes that I find to be not very accurate, though. Both of the pythagorean modes, where you take several measurememts and it generates a distance using geometry. For that to be accurate, you need to take the measurements from the same point in space. And it can be a real challenge to do just by holding it. It really needs some sort of tripod mount for that to be good. When testing on a wall that was 2.5m wide, the results were centimeters off the actual distance. It'd be even more than that if the distances measured were bigger. There's just too much error when trying to manually taking measurements from the same point in space.The main laser distance worker perfectly though, it's only the pythaogorean modes that aren't that good. And it's really only because you have to hold it. The actual function of those modes perform as expected. If you can figure out how to take measurements from the same point in space, it would be very accurate.Overall, it's a great tool for taking easy and accurate point to point distances.
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Ruler® 2.0

title: It will do the job for what I need.
content: I'm well pleased with this little unit. I bought it specifically to use as a laser level. I'm building an outdoor G-scale model railroad on an elevated trestle track. I'm planning on using this laser level to make sure the track is level. I haven't yet used it on the actual project yet, but I have tried this laser level indoors. I'm pleased with the very bright laser light. I set it on a stool using it's own level bubble to project horizontal laser beam to a wall about 12 feet away. I measured the line where it hit the wall and it was 29 inches from the floor. Then I moved the stool over to where I had just measured and pointed the laser in the opposite direction (back to where I had started). I made sure the level was a centered as I could get it. Then I went back and measured the height of the laser line. This time it measured 30 inches. I tried this several times trying to use the bubble to level the laser as best I could. I got varying results. But typically within an inch or less going back and forth. So it's not going to be right on the money. The reason for this is because the level bubble just doesn't provide a precise enough measurement for insuring that the level if perfectly level. But I can work with the one inch error over 12 feet. In fact, I only plan on using this for 8 foot spans which might do a bit better. I mainly bought this for the laser light which works very well. I might be able to attach it to something with a more refined bubble level on it to increase the accuracy. I think it's just a matter of making sure the tool is perfectly level. The Bubble that's on it just doesn't provide that kind of precision. But for $13 I'm not complaining. The laser light beam is strong and produces a nice fine line, so that's worth it right there.Also, I tried this outdoors in sunlight and it doesn't do very well because although the light seems bright indoors, it just can't compete with sunlight. However, to over come this I am using a reflector (from one of those driveway reflectors you can purchase at any hardware store). Even on a sunny day you can see the laser beam against a driveway reflector. So holding that up where you expect the beam to be sure reveal the beam even on a sunny day. Although even that is faint, but you can see it. At dusk the beam shows up naturally with no problem.For $42 I give it 5 stars. It will serve the purpose I bought it for.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: An outstanding drill crippled by automatic reverse
content: The build quality is exceptional and the same holds true for the PCB drill bits. I would have given this 5 stars, however the automatic reverse is more than an annoyance - it's a hinderance. The drill does work flawlessly for drilling through copper clad boards (PCBs), however due to the automatic reverse "feature" it takes me considerably more time to complete that it otherwise would. This could be rectified with a firmware update to allow the user to chose whether the drill powers off (desired behavior) or goes into reverse (maybe; I'll haven't disassembled the drill yet to see what components it has inside - if it's using strictly TTL to accomplish this without a microcontroller, then there is nothing that can be done).That being said, the drill is otherwise very well made. It has weight to it - not so much that it becomes uncomfortable to use, but just enough that it doesn't feel cheap and flimsy - and runs an extremely long time on a single charge. As a frame of reference, I have used it to drill through roughly 27 copper clad boards that are about the size of a PCB for a guitar pedal (approximately 2" x 4", or 5mm x 9.5mm) and it is still at a full charge. Each hole was drilled twice - once for the 0.4mm pilot hole and another for the final desired size (0.7mm - 1.1mm).If the drill could be altered/modified to prevent automatic reverse, I would say this is well worth the price. Even despite that flaw, this is my go-to choice for drilling small holes through this type of material (copper and fiberglass). The PCB drill bits do get noticeably duller, but not to the point that they significantly impact my workflow.
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Mini Electric Drill™

title: A great tool that performs great.
content: I have used the Almighty for approximately 1 month on various hobby projects requiring micro drilling and it has replaced my Dremel tool and my Proxxon Micromot for precision drilling. The tool is solidly constructed with a weighty but compact design. The torque is sufficient to drill most hard plastics and some wood without catching or snagging. I have not attempted metal yet. The bits are sturdy and have not broken after a month of regular use. The battery charge has been very good and it charges quickly, usually within 30 minutes for enough power to resume work. Overall, this is a 10 out of 10 for any hobbyist that works with small parts and needs a high level of control and precision.

title: NOT a Digital Tape Measure, but rather a digital Laser measure with a regular tape measure.
content: If you're looking for a less expensive alternative to the Reekon Digital Tape measure, keep on looking. This has a regular manual tape measure with a built-in Laser Measure. While it has some nice save features when you're using the Laser Measure, it won't record any measurements you take with the tape.That aside, lets give the Pros  Cons PROS-Digital Display-USB-C rechargeable-Tape has BOTH Inches  Metric on BOTH sides of the blade-Laser Measure built-in, don't have to carry a separate Laser Measure-Saves measurements taken with the Laser AND will do area calculations (Area, Volume of a room, Metope Area, Pythagorean)-The Hook has magnets, which can really come in handy.-Auto-Stop function on the tape, which I much prefer.-Solid build, feels good in the handsCONS-Won't save measurements made with the tape measure-Only a16' tape-Blade is a little thin in order to accommodate the Laser, but it's also only a 16' tape...so not a huge ConMy kids got me this, thinking it was basically the same as the Reekon T1...I didn't tell them anything different. But I also didn't have a Metric tape or ruler, so it's still a nice addition to the shop...and the Laser Measure doesn't hurt. Overall, a decent little device...one of my better Father's Day gifts.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Small but mighty
content: I have a plastic storage container, where I keep some feeders for my leo, but I was having issues with ventilation. This drill helped keep my critters alive! I removed the container's lid and used a thin, so that my roaches don't escape, but thick enough bit to encourage air flow. At first, I was using it incorrectly and found the drill stopping on me many times. But after reading the manual carefully, it's advised to press the drill gently over and over again, in intervals, until satisfied. In my case, it was until I punctured the lid to create perfect holes. After I got the hang of it, I created ventilation holes on all of my feeders containers and spare containers.NOW to the best part. If you're wondering if this little drill is powerful enough to drill through bone, it is! I'm a taxidermist (read bone cleaner), and I love to recycle bones from passed on critters. I used the drill to create an earring from a possum tooth and the drill worked perfectly to create the hole where the earring would hang from. It took some time, since bone is much denser, but maybe after 15 mins, and gently pressing the drill against the tooth, it punctured through! My next project is a windchime, so I'm extremely confident and happy with the power of this little, but mighty drill.

title: Handy little vacuum
content: I bought this vacuum for quick touch-ups on my car but found the blower function to be even more useful. We have hardwood floors throughout our house and also have 2 dogs. Dog hair is a constant battle, but with this vacuum I can blow out the hard-to-reach areas (under the dining table, stair treads, etc.). Once the hair is out in the open, I can vacuum it up. Pretty powerful blower, especially if you remove the filter. The vacuum function is adequate for touch up's, but I don't think it would work for a full car interior. Seems to be very well made for the money. 2 thumbs up!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Great little Vacuum
content: I recently purchased the Almighty DustBuster, and I couldn't be happier with my decision! This little powerhouse is a game-changer for keeping my car and home clean. The 14000PA suction power is seriously impressive, making quick work of dirt, pet hair, and crumbs. I love the added bonus of the LED light, which helps me spot those hard-to-reach areas in my car and under furniture at home.One of the best features is its versatility - it's not just for cleaning carpets and upholstery. The air duster function is incredibly handy for getting rid of dust from delicate electronics and keyboards. Plus, it's cordless, so I can easily move around without being tethered to an outlet and includes a usb C charging cord with it. You just need your own block or outlet.And let's talk about noise level - or rather, the lack thereof! This vacuum operates quietly, so I can tidy up without disturbing anyone or my pets.Overall, the Almighty DustBuster has exceeded my expectations. It's efficient, convenient, and makes cleaning a breeze. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a reliable handheld vacuum for their car, home, or office.

title: Great Light for any purpose
content: I bought this set of The Almighty Flashlight Duo lights because lately we've had some blackouts. I found these flashlights fantastic, 1. because they are rechargeable, 2. they are very bright and the brightness can be changed including the strobe part of it. 3. A life saver.  I just came back from a long trip to Japan and Vietnam and brought one with me. Little did I know that I would use it in one of the 5 strars hotel we were staying. I don't know if some of you know this, but in SE-Asia high end hotels come with a master switch which, if pushed, all the lights including the A/C will turn off. Well I did push the swith... and at 11pm all the power shut down. Unfortunately, the master control switch in the ceiling malfunctioned and we had no power for over an hour until the maintenance people came to the room and fixed it.  Thanks to this flashlight we were able to have so much light in that bedroom that it allowed us to read our books while waiting for the maintenance people. I will not leave home without it and keep the second one in my car in case of an emmergency breakdown. I highly recommend these powerful lights.
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Flashlight Duo™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Checks all of the boxes
content: Awesome flashlight! Easy to switch to strobe mode and very light and compact. When I teach womens self defense classes, I advise people to get a flashlight with a strobe mode that doesn't require you to cycle through all of the settings. This one, once it's on, you just have to press the on button twice fast to get the strobe. So far it has come in handy when walking the dog early in the morning in the dark and crossing busy intersection. Even with a crosswalk light being on "walk" people will still turn right on red right in front of me and refuse to stop as I am walking. Switching the light to strobe mode stops impatient drivers right away, it's magic! I am getting another one and replacing the heavier emergency flashlight (backup) in my 10 essential kit for hiking and backpacking.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: The Ultimate Measuring Companion
content: Nobody really loves getting out the old measuring tape. It's easy to miscount the tiny lines, and if you need anything more complicated than a simple length, well, good luck. But this digital measuring tape tackles all those annoyances.It's got everything you'd expect in a quality tool. A sturdy 16-foot tape measure and a bright digital display for measurements down to 1/16th of an inch. For me, the real magic starts with the laser. It's capable of stretching up to 196 feet. It automatically calculates area, volume, and even tackles Pythagorean measurements for those tricky angles. And if you're constantly juggling a notepad, the ability to save up to 30 measurements will get you covered. Plus, it's got the auto-calibration feature, so you know you're getting accurate results every time.Now, the instructions aren't the greatest, so it might take a bit of experimenting to get the hang of all the features. I had trouble finding the power button at first as it was hidden behind the sliding charging port. And while the tape measure itself feels solid, the plastic belt clip leaves me a little worried about its long-term durability, not that I often use it. But here's the thing, once you get used to this, it's tough to go back. It's super convenient, saves time, and makes you feel like a measuring pro. Whether you're a seasoned builder or a DIY-er like me, and even if you're worried about dropping it (which happens a lot with me) the protective molding seems like it could take a beating. This digital measuring tape is definitely worth checking out. And, it's rechargeable with a USB-C cable, so no more scrambling for batteries!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Excellent value - matches or exceeds most features of top Bosch model
content: I needed to level a fence and, short of using a length of clear tubing filled with water to use as a level, I decided to invest in a good laser level. Big box stores in my area mostly have Bosch with primitive models reasonably cheap and pro models well over my budget. Pro models had all the features I wanted, but prices were hard to justify. After doing some research, I am very happy to find this model from The Almighty 360 Level. This has all the right features for advanced amateur like me: 3-plane laser level, green laser diodes from Osram for excellent visibility, rechargeable battery with USB-C charging. Bag, charger and laser target plate are a nice bonus. Overall this matches or exceeds the top model from Bosch. I quickly checked square with aluminum speed square and level with traditional Stabila level - everything looks perfect. I used speed square and traditional level for all my projects - this is good enough accuracy for me. Osram LEDs are as good as it gets in terms of LED quality, so I think the level will last me a long time with moderate use. Laser levels had come a long way in recent years and now can replace the square and the level for most of my home projects.

And then I proceed to the punch line.. it’s not the laser it’s the user. 😂

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Bright green lines and great carrying case.
content: Purchased to replace my old point laser level. Now I no longer have to rotate my level to find the level point at different locations. Worked great on the first project building a deck with a screened in porch.The green laser light is extremely bright in indoor conditions. Outside, on a bright day, just like other lasers it can be difficult to see, but no problem with a little bid of shade. I also, purchased the detector, so that helped in the bright conditions as well.I liked how simple it is to use, being able to toggle through the three vertical (1) and horizontal (2) line formats easily. The automatic self leveling and self leveling and out of level mechanism works well. I liked that a lot, as wellI really like the carrying case. It is a hard case that secures the unit neatly inside for safe storage and transport.
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty 360 Level™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Great price and easy to use
content: I purchased this laser level after doing some research on red vs green, standard line vs 360 and price. Name brands run almost triple the cost for the same functionality! I needed to hang some curtain rods and rather than using a regular level and marking up the walls, I thought the laser would make things a lot easier and it did. The curtain rod is straight and doesn't dip and the distances are equally set between rod supports. The laser level really made hanging things so much faster since I did not have to rely on using a ruler or T-square to make sure everything was angled correctly.I hung the curtain rods midday when the sun was bright and hot and the line was bright and visible. I read that the red lasers were harder to see in daylight which helped my decision. Also, the 360 laser allowed me to hang curtain rods on multiple areas of the house such as the dining room and living room in one swoop since it was able to project the line all around. No need to readjust and set up. I just placed the laser on a tripod in between both rooms.Another reason I chose this model was the lithium-ion battery and USB-C charging. One con that I experienced is the magnetic mount. It is extremely strong if you are placing the laser directly on a flat clean magnetic surface. It takes quite a bit of force to pull it off. Unfortunately, it is not strong enough to attach to studs in the wall. If it does grab hold of a stud, a simple tap would send it falling to the floor. I would not recommend using the magnetic mount unless it is directly attached to a beam of some sort. Other than that, extremely useful tool!I plan on using this later when I put in a deck in the backyard so everything can be lined up nicely. Glad I purchased this. I should have gotten this sooner!
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty 360 Level™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Works very well and the case is awesome!
content: I bought this for a leveling project I had to do and also for a outdoor survey job at my house. This worked very well and is plenty bright indoors and works decently well in sunlight. The case, is designed very well and also has space for the optional receiver, which I purchased, and the adapter I use to fit the laser on my tripod. Outdoors, the receiver sees the laser when the human eye cannot, so this gets the laser out about 50' in direct sunlight. Indoors, when framing a house and setting lights, receptacles, or wall switches, it'll get from one end to the other as long there are not obstructions.

product.name: The Almighty 360 Level™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Very accurate and affordable
content: I just got this today and it was delivered in 1 day. The hard case is definitely a plus but the main reason I am writing this review on the same day is for a few reasons. 1. I set the level up in my basement and checked it for accuracy in several areas for both plumb and level. I went to an area where it was slightly visible and it was right on. I've added some photos showing different areas and showing that it's plumb and level. 2, I have been doing construction for over 30 years and I spend days researching and this does everything that the other brands do at 1/3 the price. I couldn't see buying a name brand that's black and yellow for triple the price or blue one at a triple price. It is very bright indoors and I will get the transmitter eventually and I did order 2 pairs of glasses for it today due to needing eye protection because it is a bright laser and need my help to be protected as well. 3. I like that every laser can be independent, i have a 10' service door to install, I-beam installation coming up with brick and block work, backsplash in a kitchen and remodeling 2 bathrooms and finish a basement. 4. It is very user friendly. I can say you will need a tripod that moves up and down at the point of where the laser level screws into the tripod to make adjustments easier and more accurate. This laser is well worth the money for the price and features.

This is useful for setting outdoor tiling at a set slope for drainage.Still, when I purchased the unit, I was somewhat skeptical if the laser lines from both sides would be properly aligned and would be consistent with the digital tilt angle. So, I tested the unit against my spirit levels and a well-regarded laser auto-level at zero slope. I was favorably impressed with the agreement that I saw.Also, rather than using alkaline batteries, I liked that the unit uses an internal lithium battery that can be recharged with a typical phone charger.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Very Accurate Measurements and Many Features.
content: I ordered this mainly for the laser measure ability, as well as the associated accuracy. The other features are probably going to go unused by me. However my partner, who works in the architectural field, spotted it and ran through the features and was impressed with it to the point where it has now become her daily carry, which I feel is certainly a recommendation for it. I have been promised a ‘basic laser tape measure’ for my needs to replace it - though it’s yet to appear… What I did like about this unit is the covers that slide over the plug ins to stop debris getting in and the general sturdy build of it, I feel it will withstand being dropped onto a hard surface without falling apart. The laser function worked well and can be directed from either end of the tool. Of course you can use it as a regular spool tape measure, which works smoothly. I found the instructions a little unclear, partly due to my understanding of the more technical features and also because the translation to English was a little lacking. My partner had its functions figured out quickly and only had to refer to the instructions a couple of times, so if you have used this type of device before or at least know the basics of what you require it to do, you will be fine. For my basic needs I certainly overstretched my understanding and patience but my partner is using it almost daily and finds it accurate and useful for her calculations at work.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: The most thorough kit of precision bits I've found so far
content: I've purchased other precision bit kits before, but usually for specific projects, resulting in a rather eclectic collection of bits of varying degrees of quality and usefulness. When I saw this kit and read the list of the contents, the idea of having every precision bit that I could possibly ever need all in one portable case was quite appealing. I was concerned with how it would handle being shipped, but I'm pleased to report that the manufacturer also took this into consideration. Not only is the plastic case toleranced to hug all of the bits snugly, the manufacturer included removable foam inserts that held everything completely in place. The inserts are designed to be disposed of but, given that I may occasionally travel with this set, I've retained them inside the case. They don't harm anything, and can only help, so why not? The tool handle and accessories are all easy to hold and as ergonomic as possible, and the bits are all cleanly machined with good edges and geometry all around. I've used several bits already to take apart a meter that needed repair and all have performed exactly as expected. I'm not a hundred percent sold on the outer clasp, as the magnets are only so strong, so if I do travel with it, I'll throw a rubber band or some hook and loop around it to make sure it stays shut. It looks great on my bench, and it has allowed me to clear a bunch of space previously occupied by the other smaller kits that this replaces. That's a win in my book! This would make a useful gift for the electronics tech in your life (but check with them first, just in case they've already found this kit!).

content: My shop isn't very large, and yet, I honestly work with a great variety of media and fabricate items that incorporate a bit of everything.I find myself often repairing small electronic items such as PC boards and tools and also custom building electronics for animatronics and/or custom pieces for the shop/home.This kit honestly surprised the heck out of me!The amount of design and detail that went into just the folding case is extremely advance required a huge amount of work. Everything is clearly marked and has it's own individual place, precisely laid out for ease of access and organization. Really top notch work.The cordless driver arrived charge but after using, was very easy and fast to re-charge with a USB-C cord, which was included.The driver indicates charge status and there are three adjustable power/torque settings, all quite perfect for small, precise work.The assortment of drivers is overwhelming, I really can't find anything that ISN'T included, it really seems to include anything you might need for a cell phone repair, PC board repair/replace/removal and just about everything in between.There are even very high quality extensions which can come in very handy if working within a deep housing.It's really a complete kit and it's staying atop my workbench, placed amongst the tools I always keep within arms reach.I truly can't find anything I would recommend replacing or changing within this kit, as even the foam pieces that secure the internal components when closed are precision cut and fit with absolute attention to detail.The case is very strong, quite sturdy, and kind of has a Marvel Comics X-Men design.The driver also have a positionable thumb rest on the back, making it comfortable for both large and small hands. A detail which didn't really need to be incorporated but was done for the user's comfort and convenience.The entire kit folds up so nicely and there is even a small X-Men looking magnetic button to secure it closed. It slides off with an easy sliding motion, so don't lose it.I would definitely recommend this kit and I have absolutely no doubt you'll be as impressed as I am.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: The perfect square.
content: Oddly enough levels are not level, and squares are not square. Say what? Try taking a box full of levels at your local big box store, take one out and lay it across something, then turn it around 180 degrees and place it on the same spot; is the bubble in the same position? If not, then the level is not level. I found a huge box with only a couple of levels in the whole box that were level.So, what does that have to do with this square right angle clamps. That is where the second part of the equation comes in, levels are not level, and squares are not square. If you have a square that has a riveted handle attached to it, check it by placing it along a flat fence, draw a line on the table (with a piece of paper on it of course), then flip it over and check the line. You would be surprised at how many of them are not square. Now, something that is a square that is a fixed casting, without a separate riveted handle for example, they are usually perfectly square.So, not only is this set made by a set of squares that are cast all as one piece, it also has convenient locking tabs to hold your work pieces. Getting perfect 45's in the corners is one challenge, but getting them properly clamped is another challenge. You can't use picture frame clamps on everything. This set has a broader face on it then the typical picture frame set. These are well made, do a fantastic job, yes, they are perfectly square, and won't break the bank. Highly recommended.

title: Compact power
content: I ordered this for my husband who travels a great deal for work and goes to every country under the sun. So, lots of different plug in types. He was a Samsung, I have an iPhone…so also different cords.First, let me talk about the construction and the boxing. The box is solid. It doesn’t come in anything flimsy so it’s protected during shipping. Inside the box the three adapters and the main “body” (which I found similar in size to a standard tape measure). This thing has some weight to it. It’s smooth to the touch no odd textures.Secondly, it has multiple ports for cords and a lightning port for fast charging, also a type C port which is what my iphone 16 cords need. At one point we had my phone, my husband’s phone and a vape plugged in and all were charging at the same time.Lastly, this change chargers FAST, I can allow my special needs son to play on his cell phone at dinner in public the entire time since I know if his phone dies I have a battery back up to head off the inevitable melt down that comes alone with a dead battery.Overall, well made, holds a charge well despite having multiple things charging (will charge 5 things at the same time while plugged in to a wall) and is compact enough to slip in a car glove box or my purse.Would absolutely recommend if you are in need of battery backup while out and about in town and makes international travel much easier!

title: Works quite well, wish it had more attachments
content: So I got tired of using cans of air whenever I needed to take something outside to blow out. Figured I would give this a try, and for the most part it works well!Charges with USB-C and can be charged with a fast USB-C charger, which is nice. Comes with everything you need. The attachment on the nozzle is magnetic, which makes it really easy to take on and off but it did get knocked off while I was using it once. The battery drains fairly quickly, but it still was enough for me to spray out a couple of fans I had in the house and still had 50% left.I have attached a couple pictures of a fan that was dusty in the house, showing a before and after. It (mostly) got a lot of that dust off that was baked in there all winter long. I was impressed. I wish that it came with a very narrow attachment so I could really get in there with it like a can of air would have. I also found the power button on the bottom a little difficult to use (it is a long press and then again to turn it off, but sometimes the light on the bottom goes off and on). Speaking of, it would have been WAY better if the light was on the front of the fan so you can shine it on what you are blowing. Putting it on the bottom seems like a waste.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Accurate Laser Measuring "Tape"
content: I needed a new Measuring Tape and I wanted one that was really wide so I could extend it out a great distance for measuring when I had no helper. I came across the Almighty Tape Measure and was intrigued. A Laser measure that is accurate? And goes over 100 feet? I had to try it. It works just as advertised. You just point the red laser dot on something you need to measure and almosty instantly you get a reading down to fractions of an inch. It measures what I have always called an "inside measure" meaning you place the back of the Measure against one surface, then aim the dot and press the red button to measure. It works real well. Especially for longer measures.I also like that the display is backlit. That is so helpful. Another bonus is a standard measuring tape is included. Nice and smooth action with both Standard and Metric markings, on both sides. I am used to a tape you had to lock at your required distance to keep it from retracting. This one keeps a light brake on all the time, so it just stays where you pull it out. Then a button retracts the tape at any speed you want. Slight pressure and it comes back slowly, press it all the way and it comes back in a hurry.All in all I am very impressed. This is the "tape" I will use for all my jobs.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Perfect for fast measurements in large spaces when you don't need perfect accuracy.
content: The key question is, how accurate is it? This instrument combines both a conventional tape measure, a long one, plus the laser diode measurement, plus a digital readout for the tape measure. That means you have three ways to measure a distance. The simple button configuration means that this is a highly "modal" device. By changing modes, the function of the buttons also changes. This is a bit clumsy and means that for maximum effectiveness you need to keep the manual with you. OR, you can simply use the tape in the conventional way. Build quality is good, but the reliance on plastic means that the first time you drop it might be your last. So again, how accurate is it? Once you learn that the laser is not measuring from the front of the device, but from its REAR, you can check its result with the actual tape, and find that there is an error, almost a constant error, of perhaps 5/16th inch. So you'll not want to rely on this laser if you are, say, cutting wood. But that's why the physical tape is in your hand too. As you use it, you'll find yourself adeptly switching between various measurement modes according to your need for accuracy. The unit is heavy and clumsy to handle, and even though it has a lanyard provision, it's not adequate. What it needs instead is a sturdy belt clip. Still, for the money, it's worthwhile. But do shop around -- there are MANY of these models available. This is a good one, but make an informed decision.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Great Angle Finder
content: This Coobeast Digital Angle Finder is a great little gadget at a great price. It is very small, about 2" square, and light, and comes in a small case that can be worn on a belt. I got this mainly for its angle finding function. In the default angle mode, the device measures absolute angles relative to level, but it can be set to measure relative angles. I am working on a small cabinet in my motorhome, and both the front and bottom of the cabinet are not square with the rest of it (see attached photo). This makes it necessary to measure angles in order to cut the panels for the inside. With this angle finder, I can measure the angle of the bottom and sides in order to correctly cut the panels.The Coobeast Angle Finder can also be used as a laser level, with the option to activate the laser line on the left, the right, or both sides of the device. In addition, all four sides of the unit are magnetic, with a fairly strong level of magnetism. Finally, the angle finder is rechargeable, so there is no need to worry about batteries. This is a great little device. I am sure the lasers are not as powerful and the unit is not as rugged as some similar devices, but it is not nearly as expensive as some of those other devices. This is a great little angle finder for anyone who needs to measure angles or needs a laser level but does not want to spend a lot of money.
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Rechargeable Digital Level™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Accurate, Compact, and Perfect for Woodworking!
content: The Rechargeable Digital Angle Gauge has been an excellent addition to my woodworking tools. It’s compact, lightweight, and incredibly easy to use. The digital display provides highly accurate angle measurements, which makes it perfect for setting up my tablesaw and ensuring precise cuts every time.I love that it’s rechargeable, so I don’t have to worry about constantly replacing batteries. The magnetic base is strong and securely attaches to metal surfaces, making it ideal for aligning blades and fences. It’s also quick to calibrate and provides consistent readings, saving me time and frustration during projects.If you’re a woodworker or DIYer looking for a reliable, user-friendly angle finder, this tool is a must-have. Highly recommend for its precision, convenience, and durability!Just remember to remove it from the blade when using it on your saws!
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Rechargeable Digital Level™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Strong Lazer Light And Charges Up!
content: This little guy just feels nice as it fits into the palm of your hand. As you read instructions you will see that a quick press of the on/off turns it on, press again and right lazer comes on, press again and left lazer comes on. Press again and both lazers come on. And one more time and both go off. A simple cycle and easy to follow.Once on, the angle will show up on the well lit large screen. There is an arrow on both the left and right side of screen. If screen shows "00.0 degrees and left arrow will be at bottom and right arrow will be at top. If you are too high on the left the left arrow switches to the top so between the large readout of degrees and the arrows you can see quickly when your level.You can also stick the lazer level on any metal that holds a magnet as all 4 sides has a strong magnet on this little gem.The red lazer is quite strong and capable to see what your doing. I do like that in addition to the initial lazer mark on the wall there is a horizontal lazer line to further line up what is level. This is true for both sides of the lazer projections.As long as you know your surface the level is on is level you can lay it down on it's back and the lazer will act like a plumb bob giving you a straight line from top to bottom. You will get an error message but as long as your suface is level it doesn't matter when you get a vertical line.The numbers will flip as you turn the level over so the readout is always right side up.All in all, they really thought this guy out to give a simple but accurate idea of what is level and to mark things like pictures to get them straight as an arrow.Finally, to not need batteries is a real plus, I love that. Just charge it up!This guy deserves 5 stars!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Excellent DIY Laser Level Guide Tool
content: This veey cool laser-level guide tool is an excellent value, and will be especially handy for those projects where you need to look at multiple levels and sections of a room and/or DIY project to make sure everything is in level sync. I am an amateur woodworker and an artist who hangs a lot of pictures, so there are other reviewers here who can provide a more useful analysis on how to use all the features available with this tool and how it can help with the quality finishing of a construction project, but I will add a few additional observations from a different perspective. Well made, two charging batteries that hold charge well and one can charge while you continue working; multiple options for mounting on a tripod or using the provided holder, and well-packaged in a portable, padded carrying case with a detailed manual to guide you. I was waiting to start a project where I was planning to use this, so in meantime just provided a simple picture of unit sitting on the floor to give an idea of the size, position and bright appearance of the laser lights. Note especially to those who have not used a laser like this before, or have small children, pets around - the laser lights are quite intense and can quickly cause eye damage if pointed directly into the eye, so do not operate around other adults, children who can not stay clear of the light beams, no pets. Since there are lasers projected off of multiple sides of the device, be sure to totally read up on how to operate, turn the lights on/off, etc., since it is easy to move it around and accidentally shine one of the lasers towards someone's face. Wonderful product!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Much better than its cheaper counterparts
content: There's a lot to like about this gizmo compared to the cheaper versions. First off it has left and right laser beams, which can be set for left, right, or both. It also has magnets on all 4 sides (actually the corners) so you can attach it in any orientation, which makes the laser beams even more useful. The laser beams provide a distinct leveling line on drywall, fine for inside but not potent enough for outside. I no longer do any woodworking, but would certainly like to have had this for setting various saw angles on my radial arm saw, since my mechanical angle measure could not be zeroed. As you change the angle the readout continuously changes. Another plus is its use of a rechargeable battery with a standard USB-C charge port (which means most cell phone chargers will recharge it). I don't know how long a charge will last, but there is a battery charge indicator on the display. The display is easy to read and inverts automatically once you pass 90 degrees. The read out options are degrees or percent. A thin aluminum band has been pressed in around the perimeter, providing a better surface than plastic alone would provide. Finally, the price point is only about $10 more than the cheaper versions that don't have the integrated battery, laser beams, 4 sided magnets, or metal band. I recommend it as being the more versatile and durable choice.I should note that the electronic technology used is a solid state accelerometer (which senses the acceleration towards the gravitational center of the Earth that counters the centripetal force of Earth's rotation to keep us on the planet). The same technology is added to cell phones (since it's so cheap). Directions are provided for recalibrating the sensor if it is off by more than 0.2 degrees, but I haven't found it necessary (yet). Since accuracy is plus or minus 0.2 degrees, don't expect perfectly level to necessarily read 0 degrees. That would require more expensive technology than use of an accelerometer. In any case, it's still going to be more accurate than the eyeball method using a bubble level.
