store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: The Ultimate Measuring Companion
content: Nobody really loves getting out the old measuring tape. It's easy to miscount the tiny lines, and if you need anything more complicated than a simple length, well, good luck. But this digital measuring tape tackles all those annoyances.It's got everything you'd expect in a quality tool. A sturdy 16-foot tape measure and a bright digital display for measurements down to 1/16th of an inch. For me, the real magic starts with the laser. It's capable of stretching up to 131 feet. It automatically calculates area, volume, and even tackles Pythagorean measurements for those tricky angles. And if you're constantly juggling a notepad, the ability to save up to 30 measurements will get you covered. Plus, it's got the auto-calibration feature, so you know you're getting accurate results every time.Now, the instructions aren't the greatest, so it might take a bit of experimenting to get the hang of all the features. I had trouble finding the power button at first as it was hidden behind the sliding charging port. And while the tape measure itself feels solid, the plastic belt clip leaves me a little worried about its long-term durability, not that I often use it. But here's the thing, once you get used to this, it's tough to go back. It's super convenient, saves time, and makes you feel like a measuring pro. Whether you're a seasoned builder or a DIY-er like me, and even if you're worried about dropping it (which happens a lot with me) the protective molding seems like it could take a beating. This digital measuring tape is definitely worth checking out. And, it's rechargeable with a USB-C cable, so no more scrambling for batteries!

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Super handy for taking measurements in odd spots
content: I travel a lot for work and cover a large region with a wide variety of buildings and find myself often having to take measurements in awkward spaces. When I saw this tape measure I wondered if it could help make that task a bit easier so I ordered it to try out.I have to say that it definitely does make a big difference and taking measurements is so much easier overall.The measuring tape is fairly plug and play. Once it’s out of the box you charge it, make a few adjustments and then start using it.The digital display has a simple and easy to follow layout. It’s backlit for low light conditions and the measurements are quick to read at a glance. I was concerned it would be a pain to take multiple measurements of an area and read them off the display but it’s very easy and intuitive. It’s one of my favorite features with this measuring tape.The tape itself is decent and as is typical of measuring tapes will begin to bend after a certain length requiring a second person or a landmark to place it on. It’s not as easy to read as some others but I still have my manual measuring tape in my kit if I need it. It is really neat pulling the tape out and seeing the measurement pop up on the screen.So far the measurements have been accurate and I really like that you can store them for quick reference in the app.The tape also has some other neat features like calculating the area of a space and the laser is very clear and precise.In all honesty this measuring tape is just so handy and saves me a lot of time on the job.There are a few small areas for improvement:- the build isn’t the sturdiest and with a digital screen I would have loved a hard shell carrying case to protect it from my other tools in my kit- the hook at the end of the tape is typical of most measuring tapes and could be longer to avoid having it slip off since it bends easily without using itOther than that this is a great measuring tape and I am very glad I have it. It really is worth the investment and saves a lot of time; I would definitely recommend it.
reviewer.name: Anonymous
product.name: The Almighty Tape Measure™

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Works perfectly
content: This measuring tape arrived well packaged. The package included the measuring tape itself, USB-C charging cable and user manual.The device looks like made of good quality materials. It does not look like a cheap item at all.It is a bit heavier than standard measuring tapes but that is because of built-in battery and other stuff to do the digital work for you.It is easy to use. The functions are well explained in the user manual. I struggled a little bit to understand some of them but then got hang of it.I measured the distance with the tape first and then with laser. I could not find any difference between those so I can say the laser measurement is very accurate. I have used it only to measure the distance and not to measure area or volume. You can change the measurement between metric and imperial.The best part is that you do not have to install AA or AAA batteries etc in it. It comes with built-in battery so you can charge it when it goes low instead of replacing the batteries.It had some charge in it when it arrived and I have not charged it since I got it. The thing is still working fine so I am guessing the battery life is good.Overall .. I am happy with this measuring tape and at current price of $67.99, I think its a very good deal.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Digital Measuring Tape
content: The Almighty Tape Measure is not an ordinary tool. The measuring tape is made of plastic with a dense rubber covering. While it can be operated as a normal measuring tape, once turned on, it has a lot of useful features. Most notably, on the top of the measuring tape, there is a display. The display shows the exact measurement, it can save past measurements, and it has the ability to calculate area, volume or the Pythagorean theory. Additionally, the measuring tape can be switched between various units, such as feet, feet and inches, inches, or meters. The measuring tape also incorporates a laser that can be used as a measuring device. While the measuring tape is quite useful with the tech, it can also be used as a standard measuring 16ft measuring tape.I predominantly ordered the measuring tape as a time-saving tool. Since it can quickly calculate area, volume, and even the Pythagorean theory, it saves a lot of time in manual calculation. Additionally, the ability to save past measurements allows us to take multiple measurements at once without forgetting. The measuring tape is also very accurate. The digital measurement is always spot on, and when compared to laser measurement, it is also accurate. The battery life of the measuring tape is also quite noteworthy. On a full charge, it will last roughly 5 hours, and it takes less than one to charge. The durability is also exceptional. Even after dropping the measuring tape on concrete surfaces, the measuring tape and all its tech functions without an issue, and the measuring tape looks brand new. Finally, since this device must be charged, it is also convenient it arrived with a USB type C cable.Overall, I would definitely recommend this product at the current price point of $67.99. It is extremely durable and is full of very useful tech. Whether you are in a career that requires constant measurements or are an avid do-it-yourself person, this measuring tape is well worth the investment.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: A decent implementation of a general purpose digital measuring tape.
content: PROS:* The DP20 appears to be a well-built handful of a tape measure (tape length to 16 feet, laser to 131 feet). My device did not have the Bluetooth option.* All features and special functions appeared to work as advertised.* I found no issue with the measuring dependability or consistency, whether using the laser or the tape measure. It operated within the variance I expect for general purpose use (+/- perhaps 1 mm and better for the tape measure, maybe +/- 2 mm for the laser for medium lengths).* The LCD is easy to read and stays backlit for at least 10 seconds.* The DP20 is intuitive to use.* Pushbuttons appear reliable/durable. Knowing their purpose/placement takes a little getting used to.* The DP20 is rechargeable using the included USB-to-USB-C cable.* Maybe by design, but the DP20 cannot be used while it is being charged.* The tape can be locked into position with a firm action brake.* Auto shut-off after three minutes of idle non-use.* The DP20 is unlikely to turn on accidentally due to the recessed power switch.* Included user manual is comprehensive and easy to understand (English).* Manufacturer's warranty is one year.* Manufacturer contact and address information is included in the user manual.CONS:* It appears laser measuring mode always includes the body length of the measuring tape. Can't seem to find a way to exclude that, or at least make it persist while taking the measurement. Maybe it is not an option.* The DP20 is a plastic product. The beltclip is also plastic. Beware using the beltclip. I reckon the DP20 might not endure too many drops onto a hard surface.* The beltclip does not rotate.SUMMARY:This is a decent implementation of a general purpose digital measuring tape with laser. The value is fair and everything appears to function well. Do not expose the DP20 to water.

title: A Powerful and Versatile Mini-Vacuum Cleaner
content: This is quite a powerful vacuum at the high setting. Of course, as with all these vacuums, the high setting can exhaust the battery before you can finish the job, so you have to weigh that when using it. An important feature is the display that gives you good information about power, and battery left. It comes with useful accessories, and, conveniently, a bag to put them in so you don't have to hold on to the more bulky cardboard box it comes packed in. It also comes with an extra filter. This is very important, because I don't know if I will be able to get replacement filters. I am very careful with these filters because of that. You can wash them, and use a spare toothbrush to clean the filters. Dry them thoroughly outside the vacuum cleaner. You can put them on one of your forced air heater vents if you have them. The only time you need to replace the filter is if it gets a tear in it, or if you notice that it is not picking up stuff because the filter is not porous enough any longer. That has happened to me a couple of times with other products, similar to this one, but it was after years of use.

title: Great Quality, Versatile, and Surprisingly Strong — Just Be Careful with the Case!
content: This 42-in-1 magnetic screwdriver set has quickly become my favorite little toolkit for all kinds of jobs around the house, my bike, and my computer.• Perfect size: The kit is nice and compact — easy to store or carry in a bag.• Very versatile: With so many different bits, I’ve used it for furniture, electronics, and bike maintenance.• Great value for the money: You get a lot of useful tools and good build quality for a very reasonable price.• Comfortable grip: The ratchet handle feels secure and comfortable to hold, even when using a lot of force.• Solid and durable: I needed to really tighten some bolts extra snug and thought I might break it, but it handled the force perfectly and still works like new.• Fun to use: I actually catch myself clicking and spinning the ratchet like a fidget toy — the clicking sound and smooth feel are really satisfying!• The bits stay put magnetically and the ratcheting mechanism switches directions smoothly every time.• One thing to be careful of: The case stays closed magnetically, so if you accidentally drop it, it can pop open and all the bits (except the long ones secured in the lid) will spill out. I had this happen once and it was a bit of a pain to put everything back in place.Overall, this is a sturdy, compact, and super handy kit that works well and is fun to use, too. Highly recommend for anyone needing a reliable, portable screwdriver set for everyday repairs!

title: Great tape measure to have
content: The Almighty Tape Measure impresses with its versatility and accuracy. Its dual functionality as a 131ft laser measuring tool and a 16ft tape measure ensures flexibility for various tasks. The instant digital readout provides quick and precise measurements, while the unit switching feature allows for seamless conversions. store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Very accurate
content: When I first saw this tape measure, I just had to try it out. They packed a lot into this unit. Far more than I would have thought needed. The basic design of this tape measure is practical. It's thicker than the standard size for a 16-foot tape measure but that's to accommodate the laser and needed controls for this unit to do its magic.This tape measure has four modes for displaying the distance. Feet using decimal units, inches using fractions, feet/inches with fractions and metric. And then it also has functions to measure area and volume. I'm not going to get into that as I'm too old-school and still prefer pen and paper. Maybe one day I'll take the time to play with those functions.The built in laser is really nice for measuring long distances that exceed the length of the tape or just when you don't have someone available to hold the other end. Measuring the ceiling for example. The laser appears to be very accurate. I've used stand-alone lasers for measuring before. It's nice to have one built in.The rubber-coated plastic housing has a nice feel to it. It provides a good grip. But I'm not so sure about the plastic belt latch. I fear that it might easily break. Only time will tell. The tape locking mechanism is of a nice design. It holds the tape firmly, but you can still pull some tape out or push some in if need be.The black-on-black buttons are not easy to see the marking on them. Especially in low light. But in time, I will simply memorize what each button does. There is also a memory function. I've played with it but I am going to need more time to get used to all of these features. As I've said above, they packed a lot into this tape measure.It comes with a charging cable but no charging brick. The charge port is located below the power button. You have to slide up the protective cover. I like this idea better than the typical rubber plug that many devices use.The model that I received does not have Bluetooth so this one does not use an app. But there is a Bluetooth version of this unit.In conclusion, this tape measure is more suited for construction. For small projects in the shop, I would not use it. Like any tool, it has its strengths and weaknesses. But if you need to measure the distance across a room or down the lengh of a building, this tape measure is great. Especially measuring in awkward positions, this tape measure excels in that. I think it deserves a 5-star rating.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: NOT a toy, and NOT for the timid...
content: I started to wonder why this is, specifically, a "single shot" tool. The answer became clear as soon as I utilized it, for the first time...First, where it is set can be precisely determined. Once loaded, the nail is placed EXACTLY where the user intends for it to be driven. There is no way to "accidentally" drive this. The plunger handle MUST be forcefully pushed.Second, the use of safety gear is not "optional," in my opinion. My first "test" fire was a mistake (on my part). While I knew this was not intended for "soft wood," this is exactly what I used as a test material. A time-weathered post... The firing of the nail drove it with such force that a four-inch chunk was blown completely out, at about a quarter inch depth. That nail is not likely EVER to come out.I was skeptical about a nail going through anything other than thin aluminum. I was absolutely amazed to find that this will, indeed, put that nail into concrete, steel, etc. The sole limitation would be thickness. While I don't work with metals thicker than about 1/8", I have no doubts that I can fasten ANYTHING within my range of use, with this. Whatever gets fastened with this will not be moving, thereafter.Finally - and I cannot stress this enough - BE CAREFUL. While I'm not a timid individual, I immediately understood why EVERY video of this in action showed safety equipment being used (except for those wearing "flip-flop" shoes). The potential for "blowout" from the material being fastened is VERY real. I would recommend wearing work boots, when using this. Steel-toed is recommendable.Overall, few and far between are the five-star ratings I give. This will be one...If you have need of driving a nail, in a precise location. through material that would not be penetrated by a standard "nail gun" (Paslode, Hitachi, etc.) driven by pneumatic force, this is the tool for that task.Above all, I will stress one last time:BE CAUTIOUS, AND USE PROTECTIVE GEAR.

That comes in very handy when your on the job and don't have a custom charger handy you can just pug it into the truck while you use the other battery. The tripod mound has a very handy micro adjust that's much easier to deal with than modifying the tripod height.The 360 green lasers are far and away my favorite part. I can set it up in a room and not have to worry about moving it, the whole room has a very easy to see scribe line. In my opinion the green laster is a lot easer to look at all day. I have a red laser that I've totally stopped using since getting this one.The only minor complaint I have is you have to remove the whole unit from the tripod mount to change the battery. store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: 3D Laser Level for DIY and Professional Use
content: The 3D-RS12 Laser Level is an incredibly useful tool that blends accuracy, durability, and convenience into one well-designed package. Right out of the box, it’s clear that the unit is built with quality in mind. The soft-shell carrying case with pre-cut foam offers excellent protection and organization for the level and its accessories. The three bright green laser beams provide clear and crisp projections across various distances, making alignment tasks easy, even in well-lit environments. Its ability to self-level within a certain range is a huge plus, though positioning it close to level manually before activation ensures optimal performance. The unit’s ability to project horizontal and vertical planes simultaneously makes it an essential tool for various projects, whether for home renovations, construction tasks, or precise picture hanging.One of the standout features is its power system. The inclusion of two rechargeable USB-C batteries is a fantastic addition, allowing for continuous workflow without interruptions. The ability to charge the batteries via USB-C means you can use power banks, car chargers, or any standard USB charger, making it highly convenient. However, the lack of a battery level indicator could be improved, as it would help users gauge remaining power more effectively. The magnetic mounting base provides versatility, but it may not be as strong as some high-end alternatives when used on irregular surfaces. Thankfully, the device is compatible with standard tripod mounts, making it easier to secure for stable, precise applications.Overall, the 3D-RS12 Laser Level offers great value for its price, competing with well-known brands at a more affordable cost. While minor improvements could be made, such as a better battery indicator and stronger magnet for certain mounting scenarios, its performance, visibility, and ease of use make it an excellent investment. For both DIY enthusiasts and professionals, this laser level delivers precision, durability, and convenience without breaking the bank. I would rate it 4.5 out of 5 stars.

store.name: The Almighty Tools
content:
I needed a new Measuring Tape and I wanted one that was really wide so I could extend it out a great distance for measuring when I had no helper. I came across the almighty ruler 3 in 1 Digital Laser Tape Measure and was intrigued. A Laser measure that is accurate? And goes over 100 feet? I had to try it. It works just as advertised. You just point the red laser dot on something you need to measure and almosty instantly you get a reading down to fractions of an inch. It measures what I have always called an "inside measure" meaning you place the back of the Measure against one surface, then aim the dot and press the red button to measure. It works real well. Especially for longer measures.
I also like that the display is backlit. That is so helpful. Another bonus is a standard measuring tape is included. Nice and smooth action with both Standard and Metric markings, on both sides. I am used to a tape you had to lock at your required distance to keep it from retracting. This one keeps a light brake on all the time, so it just stays where you pull it out. Then a button retracts the tape at any speed you want. Slight pressure and it comes back slowly, press it all the way and it comes back in a hurry.
All in all I am very impressed. This is the "tape" I will use for all my jobs.

title: This is going to be so good for a garden project I have in the new year
content: With an upcoming garden project next year, I was needing something better to measure with other than a tape measure (rolly one and metal one) or the Measure app on iOS.  Vine God heard me, and bestowed upon me this Almighty Ruler PRO.  Now, sure, it's a tape measure, but do take the time to read the instructions as it is more complex than just pull out the tape and drag it to where you want to. This thing is equipped with a laser for precision work such as range finding and laser marking (for cutting etc.) A digital display is on hand, with memory, so you don't need to make a mental note or have a pen and paper ready, but these functions do require some learning.  Nevertheless, this is going to be very useful for the project I have looming.

First thing I did was grab this gem, give it a few cranks and found a local radio station that could let me know what was going on. It was still dark out so I used the top desk lamp to illuminate the area I was sitting in. Hour four passed and my phone was dying....but wait....THIS THING HAS A CHARGING PORT ON IT!! It doesn't charge as fast as a wall charger would, but whomever designed this needs an award and a long distance hug from me! Should the storm have been worse, this would have been the life saver, as it solar charges and you can crank the back for extra power.

title: It Does What It Says
content: I purchased the item because Hurricane Matthew was my first hurricane and I was able to see how an item like this would be useful due to my not having it despite recommendations by many preparedness websites.  I provided 5 stars because it does everything the description says it does well without having to do any of the troubleshooting. The SOS feature scared me when I turned it on because it really is "very loud." I also think the price point is very fair given the marketplace for like items. Also, the ability to charge it 4 different ways is a surprise for the size of it.  When the device is shipped there is a notice inside thanking me as a client and to test all the features immediately to ensure I am happy with the product and who to reach if not satisfied with anything.  Also, I will put this out there because I almost didn't purchase this because of others reviews; read the full set of instructions because it states the phone charging feature is only to give the phone enough juice to make emergency calls, etc. (not fully charge the phone, so can use all the apps, etc.)  My only complaint is the black surfaces show fingerprints really bad, but I am sure when I am in need of using this something like the aforementioned will be the least of my concerns.

On the other surfaces, the suction cup is strong.* MagSafe -- As to standard driving the MagSafe strength is the strength expected. No problems here.* 360° Rotation with Three-Axis Stable Support -- I thought this was rotating at the base where the twist to the suction is. But this rotates at the bar that extends to the MagSafe. It's not tight and it's not loose. Right now, it is working great.* Durable Suction Cup -- This appears to be made as specified. The suction cup isn't flimsy material. The material seems durable and seems like it will maintain to the life of the product.* Warranty -- The warranty offered seems like they care about their product. The Support team responds quickly. store.name: The Almighty Tools
title: Portable, quick inflating, and very easy to use!
content: Awesome little gadget! It's much smaller than my excellent Cannondale mechanical mini pump and easily fits in my seat bag. It's also a touch lighter (116 g for the Almighty AirBuddy vs 119 for my mechanical pump).The unexpectedly cool thing is how easy and quick it is to use. I unzip my seat bag, pull out the pump, press the button to turn it on, just press onto the valve, then double-press it to start inflating. I feel this is much quicker than undoing the strap of my mechanical pump and extending the short inflating hose. From memory, it takes 20 seconds to completely inflate my 700c x 32 tires to 60 psi.In fact, I had a slow leak in a tire recently and I could re-inflate it and get back on my bike while waiting for a red light to change. I probably inflated the tire from 25 to 50 psi.Of course, since it's battery-powered, the negatives are that a) you have to make sure it's charged and b) you can't pump tires forever with it. I've never had it run out of battery on my, but I did get the red low-battery indicator after 4 times pumping my 700 x 32 tires to about 50 psi. Compared to a CO2 inflator though (how many spare cartridges do you carry?), I think this one's a no-brainer.

content:
The only downside is that the instruction manual could have been a little easier to understand.


The plus side
1. You can use the laser to measure either from the inside or outside of the tape measure
2. You can use it as an ordinary tape measure. It includes both metric and inches on the tape
3. In my testing it's very accurate from a few inches up to the 16+ feet it can measure.

It has lots of added features to calculate area, volume and pythagoras (which calculates the 3rd side of a triangle after you take several measurements). Not sure I'd ever use these functions but no negative about having them.


Overall it's well built. Includes a belt clip and wrist strap.


A great addition to my tool box. I wish I had this years ago.

title: Small but efficient
content: This sleek, small unit doesn't look like a flashlight, but it sure does act like one. It puts out a bright light that's comparable to much bigger, more traditional flashlight, and it's size and shape makes it more accessible and easier to use in most any space. The clip on the back lets you attach this light to your clothing or other objects so that your hands can remain free when using it, and there is also a magnetic in one end so that it can also be attached to metal. This light has a strobe function for use in an emergency. And perhaps best of all, you don't have to worry about having batteries on hand for this light, as there is an included USB cable for fast charging when needed. This little light does a lot and is very easy to use, and with two lights included, its a good deal for the price.
